authors.name: Sophia Rodriguez
authors.bio: Sophia Rodriguez is a senior fashion editor at GLAM GAZE, known for her sharp eye on global runway trends and emerging designers. With a background in fashion journalism and styling, she blends editorial storytelling with real-world influence, decoding high fashion for modern audiences. Her work spans fashion weeks in Paris, Milan, and New York, bringing insider perspectives to readers. Sophia is passionate about sustainability and often highlights ethical brands shaping the future of style. At GLAM GAZE, she leads fashion coverage with a refined voice, balancing luxury aesthetics with accessible inspiration for everyday fashion enthusiasts worldwide.
authors.meta_description: Sophia Rodriguez covers global runway trends, emerging designers, and sustainable fashion with expert insight and refined editorial storytelling.

authors.name: Ava Miller
authors.bio: Ava Miller is a youth culture writer at GLAM GAZE, focusing on Gen Z trends, digital fashion, and social media influence. She captures the evolving voice of young audiences, covering everything from viral aesthetics to emerging creators redefining style norms. With a deep understanding of online culture, Ava connects fashion with identity, self-expression, and community. Her writing is fresh, relatable, and forward-thinking, making her a key voice for the next generation. At GLAM GAZE, she curates stories that resonate with young readers, spotlighting innovation, inclusivity, and the ever-changing landscape of teen fashion and lifestyle worldwide.
authors.meta_description: Ava Miller explores Gen Z trends, digital fashion, and youth culture with fresh insights into style, identity, and online influence.

authors.name: Elizabeth Carter
authors.bio: Elizabeth Carter is a style editor at GLAM GAZE, specializing in timeless elegance and modern wardrobe essentials. With years of experience in fashion publishing, she curates sophisticated looks that bridge classic and contemporary aesthetics. Her expertise lies in personal styling, trend forecasting, and luxury fashion insights. Elizabeth’s writing empowers readers to refine their personal style with confidence and intention. She believes style is an extension of individuality and focuses on creating versatile, wearable inspiration. At GLAM GAZE, she delivers polished, insightful content that helps readers navigate fashion with clarity, confidence, and a strong sense of identity.
authors.meta_description: Elizabeth Carter shares timeless style advice, wardrobe essentials, and luxury fashion insights for confident, modern dressing.

authors.name: Scarlett Hayes
authors.bio: Scarlett Hayes is a business and fashion industry analyst at GLAM GAZE, covering market trends, brand strategies, and the evolving global retail landscape. With a background in business journalism, she provides in-depth insights into mergers, acquisitions, and the financial forces shaping fashion. Scarlett translates complex industry developments into clear, engaging stories for readers. She is particularly interested in innovation, sustainability, and the future of digital commerce. At GLAM GAZE, her work bridges fashion and business, offering a strategic perspective on how creativity and commerce intersect in today’s fast-changing industry environment.
authors.meta_description: Scarlett Hayes analyzes fashion business trends, brand strategy, and global retail insights shaping the industry’s future.

authors.name: Eleanor Vance
authors.bio: Eleanor Vance is a celebrity style writer at GLAM GAZE, known for her coverage of red carpet moments and iconic fashion statements. She closely follows Hollywood and global entertainment, analyzing how celebrities influence trends and shape public perception of style. With a passion for storytelling, Eleanor blends fashion critique with cultural context, offering readers a deeper understanding of celebrity aesthetics. Her work highlights both established stars and rising talents. At GLAM GAZE, she brings glamour, insight, and a keen editorial voice to celebrity fashion, making high-profile style accessible and inspiring for audiences worldwide.
authors.meta_description: Eleanor Vance covers celebrity style, red carpet fashion, and Hollywood trends with insightful and engaging storytelling.

authors.name: Isabella Rossi
authors.bio: Isabella Rossi is a culture editor at GLAM GAZE, exploring the intersection of fashion, art, and global society. With an international perspective, she covers cultural movements, creative industries, and the stories shaping modern identity. Isabella’s work dives into how fashion reflects social change, diversity, and innovation across different communities. She brings depth and nuance to every story, connecting trends with meaningful cultural narratives. At GLAM GAZE, she creates thought-provoking content that goes beyond aesthetics, offering readers a broader understanding of fashion’s role within art, history, and contemporary culture worldwide.
authors.meta_description: Isabella Rossi explores fashion, art, and culture, connecting global trends with deeper social and creative narratives.

authors.name: Lily Adam
authors.bio: Lily Adam is a lifestyle writer at GLAM GAZE, focusing on modern living, wellness, and luxury experiences. Her work blends fashion with everyday life, covering topics such as home aesthetics, travel, and mindful living. Lily believes that style extends beyond clothing into how people live and express themselves daily. Her writing is warm, aspirational, and practical, offering readers inspiration they can incorporate into their routines. At GLAM GAZE, she curates lifestyle content that reflects balance, beauty, and intentional living, helping readers elevate their personal spaces and experiences with a refined, modern touch.
authors.meta_description: Lily Adam shares lifestyle, wellness, and modern living inspiration, blending fashion with travel, home, and mindful experiences.

articles.title: Zara Unveils ‘Vatisimo’ Collaboration with Willy Chavarria
articles.excerpt: Zara collaborates with designer Willy Chavarria on 'Vatisimo,' a collection celebrating cultural identity, community, and global contemporary fashion.
articles.content_section_1:
Zara, a Spanish clothing store that is part of Inditex, has teamed up with New York-based designer Willy Chavarria. The name of the collection is "Vatisimo," and it has to do with culture, identity, and the influence of fashion around the world. The name comes from the superlative form of "vato," which is a word that Chicanos use to talk about close friends and family. It means unity, friendship, and connection.

The collection is deeply rooted in Chavarria’s heritage and aims to bring his cultural perspective to a broader international audience while maintaining authenticity and creative integrity.
articles.content_section_2:
The ready-to-wear collection features apparel, footwear, accessories, and jewelry designed for everyone. It showcases Chavarria's distinctive aesthetic, characterized by striking tailoring, defined silhouettes, and premium materials. These include Italian fabrics, leather, cupro, denim, knits, and jerseys.

Structured pencil skirts, wide-leg shorts with precise lines, and relaxed tailoring are key elements. Also, consider denim shirt dresses, floral blouses, and silk slip dresses.The collection also includes graphic T-shirts, hoodies, and leather items like belts and bags that tell stories about different cultures through fashion.
articles.content_section_3:
Zara says that "Vatisimo" is a celebration of dignity, visibility, and individuality that shows a modern take on Chicano identity through fashion. The designs also take ideas from American workwear and vintage leisurewear, making a one-of-a-kind mix of old and new styles.

On March 26, the collection will be available in some stores and online around the world. This partnership comes after Zara announced a two-year creative partnership with designer John Galliano. His first collection for the brand is set to come out in September 2026. This will make Zara even more involved in high-concept fashion collaborations.

articles.title: Chloë Reners Wins Fashion Award at ITS Contest in Trieste
articles.excerpt: Belgian designer Chloë Reners receives special jury mention at the 24th ITS Contest in Trieste, showcasing her creative vision and rising influence in fashion.
articles.content_section_1:
The 24th ITS Contest, which took place on March 20 in Trieste, Italy, gave Belgian designer Chloë Reners the special jury mention. The award shows how creative she is and how she sees the future of fashion around the world.

The organizers said that the award is more than just a recognition; it also includes personalized support that fits Reners' creative goals. It shows that the ITS Foundation is committed for the long term. This includes mentoring, networking with professionals, and access to important industry platforms.
articles.content_section_2:
The jury praised Reners for being able to mix her knowledge with a clear and confident creative direction. In their statement, they said that her contribution was important because she was a strong female voice in a fashion industry that is still mostly male.

They praised her calmness, patience, and dedication, saying that she could one day run a fashion house. Andrea Rosso, Barbara Franchin, Carlo Capasa, Maximilian Raynor, and Sara Sozzani Maino were all well-known people in the industry who were on the jury. They all agreed that she had a unique style and good leadership skills.
articles.content_section_3:
Ten finalists from all over the world were also honored with Reners. Designers from France, the UK, Belgium, China, and the US took part, showing how global the competition was.

The finalists received a full prize package that included a ten-day creative residency in Trieste with help from industry experts. They also got to show off their work for ten months in the "Rise and Shine" exhibition at ITS Arcademy, the Museum of Art in Fashion. Each finalist also got a 10,000 euro grant to help them with their creative growth.

articles.title: Weekend Max Mara and Sebago Launch Final SS2026 Capsule Collection
articles.excerpt: Weekend Max Mara and Sebago release their final Spring/Summer 2026 capsule, reimagining the classic Docksides boat shoe with updated modern details.
articles.content_section_1:
Weekend Max Mara and Sebago have launched the third and final installment in their joint capsule collection, dubbed “A Weekend with Sebago,” for the Spring/Summer 2026 collection. The collection consists of a renewed version of Sebago’s legendary Docksides boat shoe, combining heritage and modern looks.

This concluding chapter of the partnership highlights both brands’ shared commitment to craftsmanship and timeless casual style, marking the end of a successful collaboration.
articles.content_section_2:
The renewed Docksides are distinguished by a clean white sole and are available in four colorways, ranging from leather in white and brown to soft suede in blue and khaki. A notable feature in the shoe’s design are braided laces in contrasting colors running along the sides and tied at the front.

The shoes are further enhanced by oversized tassels on the upper, which can be removed to provide a range of styling possibilities. Finally, co-branded elements are featured on the shoes, bringing together the two brands. This collection retails at 319 euros. It is currently available in store and online.
articles.content_section_3: Weekend Max Mara was originally established in 1983. What was once a casual wear brand has developed into a worldwide lifestyle brand. Max Mara’s heritage is based in Italian style. On the other hand, Sebago, a heritage footwear brand, is part of the BasicNet Group. This group consists of brands such as Kappa, Superga, K-Way, Woolrich, and many more.

articles.title: Ukrainian Fashion Week AW26/27 Highlights Cultural Continuity Amid Challenges
articles.excerpt: Amid challenges, Ukrainian Fashion Week AW26/27 in Kyiv highlights fashion as a symbol of resilience, cultural identity, and sustainable practices.
articles.content_section_1:
Ukrainian Fashion Week returned with its Autumn/Winter 2026/27 edition in Kyiv, taking place from March 12 to 15 under challenging conditions. Ongoing attacks on energy infrastructure and resulting disruptions to power and heating impacted the event, yet it continued to assert its presence as a key cultural platform.

Since its comeback in September 2024, the event has been taking place in an atmosphere of global unease, but it has proved to be a symbol of resilience, showing that fashion is beyond aesthetics. It is a cultural and economic phenomenon, deeply embedded in the fabric of Ukrainian culture.

Founder and CEO Iryna Danylevska emphasized that this season would be marked not by obstacles, but by the unshakable faith in Ukrainian fashion and its philosophy.
articles.content_section_2:
The theme of identity, whether it be national, individual, or brand, has become one of the key ideas in the event. In addition to this, another important theme has been sustainability and textile innovations.

Under the title “Art of Fashion,” there has also been an exhibition that has included innovations in terms of artistic techniques, sustainability, and innovations in materials. Designers like Bevza, J’aemme, Kseniaschnaider, Litkovska, Nadya Dzyak, and many others have showcased their works that reflect a forward-thinking but also deeply rooted philosophy.

These projects showed how Ukrainian designers are blending tradition and innovation, redefining fashion as a means of storytelling and cultural preservation.
articles.content_section_3:
The theme of sustainability was not just limited to an idea but was reflected in several design philosophies. For example, brands like Upslowuse and St. Mariia reflected themes like upcycling and zero waste as part of their design philosophies. This reflects the role of materials as carriers of cultural and historical significance.

The themes of design included transformation, layering, reinterpretation, where clothing was used to convey themes like time and self. For example, the Upslowuse collection, titled "Liminal," incorporated influences from Ukrainian folklore and demonology to give the designs more conceptual significance, emphasizing concepts like transition and tension.

Ukrainian Fashion Week, through these concepts, not only addressed the idea of sustainability as an environmental practice but as a cultural and philosophical one, emphasizing the role of fashion as something that is evolving and changing with the times.

articles.title: King Louie Advances Circular Fashion with Textile Waste Collection
articles.excerpt: King Louie launches a collection from textile waste using CIRCULOSE®, advancing circular fashion and sustainable manufacturing practices.
articles.content_section_1:
New Step Towards Circularity

King Louie a clothing brand, is making a big step toward a more sustainable future by releasing a new line made from old clothes. Almost 99 percent of the world's textile waste ends up in landfills or is destroyed every year. The brand wants to break this cycle by turning these useless materials into useful clothing.

The Spring/Summer 2026 collection from King Louie has new materials that cut down on waste and damage to the environment. The brand is still working toward a more sustainable future by using circularity, repair, and rental. The brand has been working toward a more sustainable future for two years, and now it is releasing new materials that will help cut down on waste and damage to the environment.
articles.content_section_2:
What is CIRCULOSE®?

King Louie's new collection includes the release of a new material called CIRCULOSE®. This material is made by recycling old clothes and leftover materials from production. This new material turns textile waste into a raw material, which cuts down on waste and harm to the environment.

King Louie joins the growing movement in the fashion industry to make new materials and use environmentally friendly production methods by recycling textiles into textiles. This method makes it less necessary to use new materials while still making new materials in a way that is good for the environment.
articles.content_section_3:
Comfort Without Compromise & Future Ambitions

The collection's main features are comfort and versatility, along with style and sustainability. King Louie uses CIRCULOSE® with certain fibers to make sure the recycled yarns are strong enough to last and be of high quality.

King Louie's plans for the future include a fully circular product chain in which clothes are made entirely from textile waste, so no new raw materials are needed. The company's goal is for 25% of the collection to be circular by 2030. The launch of CIRCULOSE® is an important step toward this goal.

articles.title: Zara x John Galliano Collaboration Sparks Industry Debate
articles.excerpt: Zara and John Galliano’s collaboration sparks industry debate, with mixed reactions over its impact on fashion and brand strategy.
articles.content_section_1:
From Outrage to Strategic Debate

The announcement of a two-year partnership between Zara and prominent designer John Galliano sparked various reactions in the fashion world. As announced by Zara, John Galliano is to reinterpret Zara’s archives in a series of seasonal collections, with the first available from September 2026.

Speaking of this partnership, luxury fashion expert Dr. Hakan Karaosman defined this partnership between Zara and John Galliano as a strategic move by Inditex to reach a new market segment, with young people no longer interested in traditional luxury goods.
articles.content_section_2:
Creative Tensions and Industry Criticism

However, not all responses have been positive. Designer consultant Silvia Pellegrino was critical of the collaboration. She felt that fashion was becoming too focused on business strategy rather than creativity. She questioned whether Zara’s approach to copying rather than creating was compatible with Galliano’s background as an artist.

Marketing expert Oana Leonte was concerned about Zara’s lack of archive. She felt that the collaboration may be based on “borrowing meaning” from outside creatives. She noted, however, that Zara’s record was one of short-term collaborations with creatives such as Narciso Rodriguez and Ludovic de Saint Sernin.

Linda Carlen a Swedish tailor, was concerned about the personal brand of Galliano. She felt that working with fast fashion may threaten his haute couture status.
articles.content_section_3:
Business Strategy Meets Legacy

From a business standpoint, the partnership makes sense. Elisabetta Borghi, an industry expert, said, “Zara’s sales growth has slowed in recent periods. The company’s strategy to enter a higher segment through better designs and higher-quality materials could be a step in the right direction.”

John Galliano, known for his theatrical and historically influenced designs, made his mark in the industry through his work at Givenchy and Christian Dior. His legacy has been marked by both innovative and provocative work. This partnership is an important part of his journey.

The real question now is whether Galliano can bring his visionary style to the fast-paced world of Zara. The partnership, which seems to be backed by Marta Ortega Perez, could change the face of Zara or continue to muddy the waters between high and low.

articles.title: Radiant Earth Emerges as the Defining Colour Trend for 2028
articles.excerpt: WGSN and Coloro name Radiant Earth as the 2028 Colour of the Year, with the warm earthy tone gaining momentum across global runways and fashion collections.
articles.content_section_1:
Radiant Earth Named Colour of the Year 2028

WGSN and Coloro, two trend forecasting sites, have named Radiant Earth the Color of the Year for 2028. Radiant Earth is a warm, grounded orange color that has been called a "reliable yet assertive neutral." This means that the consumer's attitude has changed.

Radiant Earth has been chosen as a step toward stability and strength in a world full of unknowns. In this situation, people are putting their long-term health ahead of short-term emotional trends. Radiant Earth has thus become a symbol of balance and confidence.
articles.content_section_2:
Radiant Earth's ability to be used in many different ways is a big reason why it has become the Color of the Year. Radiant Earth works well with organic materials, which shows that it has a strong connection to nature. It also makes modern industrial materials with high-gloss finishes feel warmer at the same time.

Radiant Earth works well as a strong statement color or a background color. Radiant Earth has been easy for designers to use in their designs, which can be anything from simple to very expressive.
articles.content_section_3:
Radiant Earth is making its mark on the fashion world, from the opulence of haute couture to the everyday appeal of casual wear and the elegance of menswear. Its influence extends beyond just clothing; accessories, too, are transformed, adding a captivating element to the overall aesthetic.

Radiant Earth will continue to be used by designers, and it will likely have an impact on future collections. It offers a realistic but modern color palette that meets changing consumer needs and design trends.

articles.title: Valentino Blends 80s Glamour with Baroque Elegance in Rome Return
articles.excerpt: Valentino returns to Rome with an 80s-inspired AW26/27 collection, combining bold silhouettes with Baroque grandeur in a tribute to its heritage.
articles.content_section_1:
Valentino returned to its Roman origins for a breathtaking Fall/Winter 2026–27 runway presentation, held at the historic Palazzo Barberini. The event took place only two months after the death of founder Valentino Garavani, which made it a very sad time for the fashion house. About 700 people came, including actress Gwyneth Paltrow, to see the brand's newest collection in one of the city's most famous Baroque settings.

Valentino usually shows its collections in Paris, but this season creative director Alessandro Michele chose Rome to honor the brand's roots since it was founded in 1960.
articles.content_section_2:
The collection was heavily influenced by 1980s fashion, with structured shoulders, cinched waists, and mini-skirts making up the bold shapes. Models walked down a runway covered in grass under Pietro da Cortona's famous ceiling fresco. This made a strong contrast between modern fashion and classical art.

Accessories played a crucial role, with bold jewelry such as large pearls and eye-catching pendants adding a touch of opulence to the overall aesthetic. Michele talked about the late 1980s and 1990s as a time of creativity and empowerment, especially for women. This was a time when fashion focused on confidence and being yourself.
articles.content_section_3:
The collection also showed off Valentino's signature craftsmanship, with pleated fabrics and draped shapes that were meant to celebrate the female form. Michele said that this way of thinking about women as strong, central figures was what she was going for.

The finale featured a show-stopping creation: a long-sleeved gown in Valentino's signature red, showcasing a striking open back. Michele acknowledged the challenge of working with such a vivid hue, yet he emphasized its significance to the brand's core, a nod to Valentino's enduring reputation for elegance and glamour.

articles.title: Wrangler x Dr. Scholl’s Shoes Capsule Collection Launch
articles.excerpt: Wrangler and Dr. Scholl’s Shoes join forces for a limited-edition collection blending Western-inspired denim aesthetics with iconic comfort-driven footwear.
articles.content_section_1:
A Heritage Collaboration Rooted in Comfort and Style

Wrangler, a denim brand, has partnered with Dr. Scholl’s Shoes, a footwear brand, to launch a limited-edition capsule collection. This is a collaboration between two iconic American heritage brands. This capsule collection is set to launch on March 24. This capsule collection reinterprets five of Dr. Scholl’s signature footwear styles, including the brand’s iconic Original Sandal.

The capsule collection offers comfort and style with new and innovative designs in footwear that are infused with colors, patterns, and materials associated with Wrangler. This capsule collection also includes a denim jacket that has been designed with leather details and Wrangler’s signature stitching with a ‘W.’.
articles.content_section_2:
Western Aesthetic Meets Modern Trends

The Dr. Scholl’s x Wrangler range is a collaboration that is inspired by a shared heritage of freedom and self-expression. It is a range that is fueled by the rising trend of Western-inspired clothing and footwear. It has many standout elements that include metallic accents, rivets, cow print calf hair, perforated leather, and denim.

The range includes a variety of products that range from wood sole sandals and clogs to new and innovative designs of the popular Time Off sneaker. The range is available online and has a price range of between $89 and $199.99. It is another collaboration by Wrangler that is a continuation of their high-profile partnerships with lifestyle brands.

articles.title: FW26 Trend: Designers Reimagine the Peplum for Sculpted Silhouettes
articles.excerpt: From Dior to McQueen, the peplum returns for FW26 with a sharper, architectural edge, redefining waistlines and volume across modern runway collections.
articles.content_section_1:
The peplum has made a strong comeback on the FW26 runways, which aligns with the new focus on defined waistlines and volume control in fashion. The new peplum styles are more precise in design compared to the soft, ruffled styles of the 2010s, which can fit perfectly in clothing items such as tailored clothing and dresses.

Jonathan Anderson went back to Dior's archives and gave the famous "Bar" jacket a new look with a sharper, more sculptural feel. The peplum is part of a larger trend toward defined waists and expressive proportions. Designers use structure to make things move and be dynamic.

Ashlyn FW26
articles.content_section_2:
Ashlyn wore a daring all-red outfit with a long-sleeved turtleneck top and a peplum hem. The heavily fringed maxi skirt balanced out the sculpted waist. It added texture and movement to the structured shape.

CFCL FW26 by Yusuke Takahashi
articles.content_section_3:
CFCL showed off a modern, stylish version of the trend with a peplum-style zip-up jersey knit jacket and a matching flared skirt. The muted neutral tone of the look made it look comfortable while keeping its elegant, sculptural shape.

Christian Dior FW26 by Jonathan Anderson
articles.content_section_4:
Jonathan Anderson designed a new version of the "Bar" jacket for Dior. It was shorter in length with a defined peplum. The look was both old-fashioned in cut and modern in texture and proportion with a mini skirt in ultra-soft shearling in candied almond.

McQueen FW26 by Seán McGirr
articles.content_section_5: McQueen designed a glamorous version with a sleeveless peplum top in pistachio floral silk mikado. It was both structured and decorated with the addition of bugle bead embroidery and high-waisted shorts.

articles.title: Wearable luxury dominates: Farfetch Index on 2026 fashion trends
articles.excerpt: Farfetch’s 2026 index highlights wearable luxury, featuring top footwear, clothing, and accessories blending style with everyday utility.
articles.content_section_1:
Farfetch's latest index, predicting the luxury wardrobe trends for 2026, draws on sales data projected through 2025, along with global search volumes. The report discusses a lot on how the world is shifting towards "wearable luxury," meaning items that are not only culturally relevant but are also functional for everyday life.

The Adidas Samba was the most searched shoe worldwide, with more than 52.3 million searches.This illustrates the shoe's evolution, from its roots in athletics to its current status as a luxury item. The New Balance 9060 followed closely behind, garnering nearly 48 million searches, a clear indication of the public's fascination with its distinctive, tech-driven midsoles.

Other popular shoes include the Onitsuka Tiger Mexico 66 and the Maison Margiela Replica.The Moncler Maya jacket was the most searched for clothing item, with over 1.1 million searches. It is known for its high-gloss finish and functional down filling.The Max Mara Teddy Coat was still a key investment piece, along with "quiet luxury" basics like the Ami de Coeur T-Shirt from Ami Paris and the Comme des Garçons Play Heart-Patch T-Shirt.

Practical luxury was the most popular type of accessory.The Longchamp Le Pliage was the most searched-for bag, with more than two million searches. People liked it because it was light and could be folded up. The Coach Tabby and Marc Jacobs The Tote were next. Prada's Symbole sunglasses were the most popular, followed by Tom Ford's Bronson sunglasses. Both were praised for their architectural, face-framing design.

Doralice Belli, who is in charge of merchandising at Farfetch, said that these products work because they don't take away from personal style; they add to it. The Index says that the most popular luxury items in 2026 will be those that fit in well with work, travel, and leisure clothes.

articles.title: Adam Pendleton and Gabriela Hearst Launch Exclusive Nina Bag Collection
articles.excerpt: Gabriela Hearst and artist Adam Pendleton collaborate on 25 unique, hand-painted Nina bags, blending art and fashion while supporting Nina Simone’s legacy.
articles.content_section_1:
Working in collaboration with artist Adam Pendleton, Gabriela Hearst has designed limited-edition bags that challenge the traditional understanding of fashion, art and sculpture. Each Nina bag is painted by hand, making it a one-of-a-kind work of art that comes with a signature. The bags have been created in celebration of the musician and activist, Nina Simone, who inspired their name.

Since its founding in 2017, Adam Pendleton has taken part in the effort to buy and preserve the Nina Simone Childhood Home located in North Carolina, making another nod to the artist whose memory he seeks to commemorate with this initiative. Funds collected during the sales will go towards the preservation of the Nina Simone Childhood Home as part of the African American Cultural Heritage Action Fund at the National Trust for Historic Preservation.
articles.content_section_2:
Only 25 individually signed and numbered bags will be made available for purchase exclusively at Sotheby’s. In addition, the collection will be displayed at the Sotheby’s Salon in New York, giving people an opportunity to see it as a piece of art. This collaboration is based on common ideas that both Hearst and Pendleton had in mind.

According to Pendleton, the collection pays homage to Nina Simone’s artistic bravery, while Hearst states the collaborative process and its essence. Thus, this partnership not only recognizes and respects the legacy of Nina Simone, but it is also a demonstration of how fashion can become an art form.

articles.title: Barcelona to Host First-Ever European Designer Fashion Summit
articles.excerpt: Barcelona will host the first European Designer Fashion Summit, bringing industry leaders together to discuss sustainability and the future of fashion.
articles.content_section_1:
Barcelona is set to host the inaugural European Designer Fashion Summit on April 13–14, marking a key moment during the 37th edition of 080 Barcelona Fashion. The event will take place at the Tinglados event space in the Port of Barcelona and is organized by the Fundación Academia de la Moda Española (FAME) and the Asociación Creadores de Moda de España (ACME), in collaboration with the European Fashion Alliance (EFA).

With backing from prominent organizations such as the Spanish Ministry of Culture, Generalitat de Catalunya, and Gran Canaria Swim Week, the summit seeks to foster communication and cooperation among the European fashion industry. Conceived as a space for strategic thinking, it will address various topics related to digital revolution, environmental revolution, and the new cultural function of fashion. Admission to the event is free for members of the industry, although registration is mandatory.
articles.content_section_2:
The two-day activity will be conducted by over twenty experts in the field, who will conduct various sessions such as conferences, interviews, and debates. The first day of the event will focus on designer fashion as an indicator of culture and its worldwide impact, while the second day will focus on internationalization, sustainability, and digitalization.

Prominent speakers include Carlo Capasa of the Camera Nazionale della Moda Italiana, Pascal Morand of the Fédération de la Haute Couture et de la Mode, Cecilie Thorsmark of Copenhagen Fashion Week, and Scott Lipinski of the European Fashion Alliance. Additional voices such as philosopher Gilles Lipovetsky, Dior Men’s sustainability head Natalia Culebras, and Pyratex CEO Regina Polanco will contribute to the discussions. Hosted by designer Juan Duyos, the summit aims to strengthen collaboration across Europe and position fashion as a force for cultural innovation, sustainability, and global influence.

articles.title: AW26 Quiet Elegance: Minimalism and Luxury Fashion Trends
articles.excerpt: AW26 fashion embraces quiet elegance, minimalism, and craftsmanship, with trends like checks and sculptural florals shaping modern luxury.
articles.content_section_1:
The fall/winter period of 2026 turned out to be very unique compared to other seasons, since designers were much more concentrated on their work and less worried about making the press buzz. After experiencing all the changes that occurred in terms of creative directors and provocative launches, this year's fashion collections became calm and well-thought-out; they consisted only of flawless clothes made by skilled designers.

Everything was framed against the background of some important events happening around the world at the moment, such as the ongoing wars in Iran and the Middle East. Therefore, most fashion brands chose not to provoke anyone and opted for subtle elegance instead.
articles.content_section_2:
Wearability became one of the dominating themes. However, despite seeming rather obvious, the designers managed to take the minimalistic approach to another level by adding subtle details and elevating simple pieces through textures, fabrics, and other elements. At the same time, many designers successfully merged classic evening wear elements with casual clothes, simplifying silhouettes while preserving their expressiveness.

Accessories had an important part to play in this shift as well, as decorative jewellery was used to complement even the plainest of looks. It was the perfect combination of simplicity and complexity that enabled people to make personal choices about how to interpret the collections.
articles.content_section_3:
Of the new trends we saw during the Fall/Winter 2019 season, one that stood out was that of checks. The timeless motif found its way into several collections, and while brands like Burberry continued developing their unique check patterns, other designers such as Chloé and Rabanne gave it new meanings. Checks were able to be adapted and worn in numerous ways, ranging from simple blouses to elaborate combinations of layers.

The final trend of the season was that of “everyday trains,” which usually accompany formal outfits but were incorporated in modern looks by designers like Khaite, Prabal Gurung, and Sandy Liang.
articles.content_section_4:
Floral brooches were another noteworthy detail, transcending the traditional uses of the motif and taking on a more sculptural and artistic appearance. The Chanel maison returned to its signature flower but with a softer and more delicate interpretation, while designers such as Dior and Simone Rocha worked with the motif itself, creating three-dimensional shapes. These small additions were in line with the emphasis on craftsmanship in the upcoming season.

All in all, the AW26 season is an indication of the industry’s development towards maturity, where integrity and innovation are valued more than spectacle.

articles.title: V&A Dundee Explores the History of the Fashion Catwalk
articles.excerpt: The V&A Dundee’s ‘Catwalk’ exhibition traces over a century of fashion shows, from intimate salon presentations to today’s global, theatrical spectacles.
articles.content_section_1:
The V&A Dundee’s exhibition Catwalk: The Art of the Fashion Show explores more than a century of fashion show history, beginning with intimate 19th-century salon presentations and evolving into today’s global spectacles. Among its highlights is a story from 1971, when Manolo Blahnik designed shoes for Ossie Clark’s London show but भूलfully omitted steel pins in the heels, causing models to wobble down the runway—an accident later praised as a new style of walking. This anecdote captures the unpredictable creativity that has long defined the catwalk.

The display of more than 100 years of fashion history demonstrates the impact of catwalk fashion on mainstream fashion and consumer behavior. According to curator Leonie Bell, catwalk fashion dictates not only high fashion but also the clothes that people end up wearing and purchasing. In addition, the event also celebrates the contribution of Scotland to catwalk fashion, from early exhibitions in Glasgow to catwalk displays by Christian Dior and current catwalk shows in historical locations.
articles.content_section_2:
Exploring the history of the catwalk, the exhibition starts with the development of "living mannequins" in the 1850s, which was used by fashion houses in the promotion of their clothes among wealthy clients. With time, fashion shows shifted from fashion salons to less traditional settings such as gardens and ocean liners, turning into powerful marketing instruments. In the mid-twentieth century, the rise of ready-to-wear clothes infused energy into fashion shows, with designers like Paco Rabanne and Mary Quant revolutionizing catwalk fashion with creative fabrics, music, and energetic performances.

This signaled an end to traditional static shows that were both restrictive and elitist. The catwalk show was no longer simply about displaying the clothes; it had also become a means of capturing changes in styles, identities, and trends within society.
articles.content_section_3:
Fashion shows in the contemporary era have come a long way and now include elements of luxury marketing combined with advanced technology in creating a multi-dimensional experience. The exhibit is a testimony to this change through events like the Chanel rocket launch show by Karl Lagerfeld, along with theatrical performances involving famous models and stunning stage designs. The invitations themselves have now transcended their functional purpose and can even be considered works of art.

Beyond the glitz, Catwalk also showcases fashion's collaborative and democratic spirit. The exhibition's focus on behind-the-scenes essentials—makeup, wigs, and styling equipment—underscores the teamwork that goes into every show. It makes clear that fashion shows aren't just singular spectacles; they're cultural events that shape and mirror the world around us. From those in the front row to those watching from afar, the exhibition invites everyone to be part of this ongoing story.
articles.image1_source: A Paul Poiret fashion show in Paris in 1910

articles.title: Tracksuit Pants Summer Trend 2026: How to Style Them
articles.excerpt: Tracksuit pants are a key summer trend in 2026, blending sporty comfort with street style. Discover how to style them for a modern spring and summer look.
articles.content_section_1: It seems that tracksuit pants are going to be the next big trend, and they are staying put regardless of the warmer weather approaching. Originally used only as workout apparel, tracksuits have become an incredibly popular item for building a casual wardrobe due to influencers, celebrities, and fashion bloggers all wearing them. The first place one thinks of when considering the best tracksuit is definitely Adidas with their distinctive three stripes, but brands like Needles from Japan and Nike should not be overlooked either.
articles.content_section_2:
With a Poplin Shirt: For a simple yet stylish spring and summer look, pair your tracksuit pants with a cotton poplin shirt. Opt for long-sleeved (perfect for rolling up) or three-quarter sleeves. Classic white or light blue poplin shirts are excellent choices that won't clash, whether your pants are a muted black or navy, or a vibrant green, maroon, or red. A black top also works well for a more sophisticated, less summery feel. For a casual-chic vibe, try flip-flops or Birkenstock-style mules. Elevate the look with loafers or ballet flats for a city-chic aesthetic. Adding a blazer over your chosen shirt can provide a touch of formality.

With a Tank Top: Another ideal spring and summer pairing for tracksuit pants is a simple tank or crop top, whether it's a classic style or features spaghetti straps. This is a perfect combination for hot days when you want leg coverage for comfort or to avoid contact with public surfaces. It's the closest you can get to wearing pajamas out while maintaining a balanced body temperature. If a tank top isn't your preference, layer a cotton shirt over it, worn open as an overshirt.
articles.content_section_3: With a Statement Jacket: Don't put your jackets away just yet! Even in summer, evenings can get cool, and rain can still occur. A statement jacket is a fantastic way to complement tracksuit pants. A trusty leather jacket or a light-colored, bright jacket can instantly elevate the look. For cooler climates, consider an animal-print jacket to contrast with the solid color of your tracksuit bottoms, creating an effortlessly stylish ensemble.

articles.title: Dwayne Johnson Turns Heads at 2026 Met Gala With Thom Browne Skirt Look
articles.excerpt: Dwayne Johnson made his Met Gala debut in a custom Thom Browne outfit inspired by Polynesian culture, sparking conversation about fashion, masculinity, and identity.
articles.content_section_1:
Dwayne Johnson made his debut at the 2026 Met Gala on Monday, turning heads with a custom all-black Thom Browne outfit that featured a pleated skirt layered over trousers. The look included a black mohair tailcoat, a white vest, a bow tie, and over 350 meters of hand-pleated silk ribbons arranged in what the designer described as a “skeletal composition.”

Johnson appeared at New York's Metropolitan Museum of Art with his wife, Lauren Hashian, dressed in a Thom Browne white halter neck gown. He also completed the appearance with a multimillion-dollar watch that drew attention on the red carpet. The actor's attire instantly became one of the night's most talked-about looks, thanks to the skirt, which prompted buzz both online and among reporters in attendance.
articles.content_section_2:
When asked about wearing a skirt on the Met Gala carpet, Johnson explained that the choice was inspired by his Polynesian heritage. He said Thom Browne’s team had initially asked if he would feel comfortable wearing the pleated skirt as part of the design. Johnson said he immediately embraced the idea because skirts, or lavalavas, are common in Polynesian culture. “The most masculine men wear lavalavas and skirts,” he explained while discussing the cultural meaning behind the outfit.

A lavalava is a traditional garment worn throughout Polynesian and Oceanic cultures and is commonly used in schools, workplaces, and even police uniforms in some regions. Johnson's comments connected the choice of clothes to a bigger conversation about cultural identity and masculinity. He said that people shouldn't judge traditional clothing based on narrow stereotypes.
articles.content_section_3:
The 2026 Met Gala's theme was "Fashion Is Art." The show that went with it, "Costume Art," looked at how clothes, culture, and the human body have changed over time. During red carpet interviews, Johnson commented on the theme and explored the concept of fashion as artistic expression. He discussed with fellow actor Colman Domingo how fashion may become "a walking expression of art." When asked if he considered himself "the art or the artist," Johnson said he thought he was "both," and that it felt like "a cool place to be in."

The event's co-chairs included Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams, and Anna Wintour.
articles.content_section_4:
This was not the first time Johnson publicly discussed masculinity and representation. Earlier this year at CinemaCon, while promoting Disney’s live-action Moana adaptation, he said that “real masculinity” means empowering and supporting women. Johnson described the story’s lead character as “a warrior” rather than a princess and said his role as Maui is centered on helping and supporting the heroine. He added that men of all ages should champion the women in their lives.

His comments at both CinemaCon and the Met Gala reflected a consistent message about redefining traditional ideas around masculinity, culture, and self-expression.
articles.content_section_5: Outside of fashion and public appearances, Johnson remains one of Hollywood’s busiest stars. He recently appeared in The Smashing Machine, which earned critical acclaim and received an Academy Award nomination for best makeup and hairstyling. Johnson also continues his role as a board member of TKO Group Holdings, the parent company of WWE. He is set to return later this year in Jumanji: Open World and is also expected to appear in Fast Forever in 2028.

articles.title: Spectacular Jewelry of Met Gala: From 550-Carat Tanzanite to Beyoncé’s Crown
articles.excerpt: The 2026 Met Gala delivered unforgettable jewelry moments, from Emily Blunt’s pearl body necklace to Sudha Reddy’s breathtaking 550-carat tanzanite masterpiece.
articles.content_section_1:
Statement Jewelry Dominated the Red Carpet

It was an event that dazzled the world when it took place in 2026 with a guest list consisting of some of the most influential people in the world like Isha Ambani, Lauren Sanchez, and Anne Hathaway, all wearing jewelry that made people gape in envy. In this Met Gala, the “Costume Art” concept was celebrated, and accessories that made the red carpet a spectacle were worn.

This year’s jewelry spotlight was firmly on statement necklaces. Emily Blunt, fresh from the press tour for The Devil Wears Prada 2, wowed in a Mikimoto body necklace valued at over half a million dollars. Its artful draping of pearls created a stunning, sculptural effect that complemented her elegant look. Anne Hathaway celebrated her enduring allegiance to Bvlgari with a regal gold laurel-inspired necklace, a tribute to her Roman-inspired gown and the eternal luxury that the brand embodies.
articles.content_section_2:
Rare Gemstones and Symbolic Designs Stole the Show

With Venus Williams being the co-chair for the event, there was brilliance on the red carpet. With her wearing a custom-made piece by Swarovski, Venus went with a neckplate that contained special symbolism to her and included a miniature version of LA's Watts Tower, as a reminder of her Californian origins.

However, it would be an injustice not to mention Sudha Reddy who graced the red carpet with her appearance while wearing her own stunning jewelry, a necklace worth an astonishing $15 million owing to the deep violet-blue tanzanite gemstone weighing 550 carats.

articles.title: SWAIA Native Fashion Week Brings Indigenous Style to the Spotlight in Santa Fe
articles.excerpt: The 2026 SWAIA Native Fashion Week gala transformed downtown Santa Fe into a showcase of contemporary Indigenous fashion, featuring visionary designers blending heritage craftsmanship with modern design.
articles.content_section_1:
Santa Fe Celebrates Indigenous Fashion Excellence

Last Saturday night, the historic Eldorado Hotel in downtown Santa Fe transformed into a vibrant street style scene, rivaling any major fashion capital. Guests dressed in stunning Indigenous-made designs and jewelry, including jaw-dropping turquoise squash-blossom necklaces, arrived in droves for the 2026 SWAIA Native Fashion Week gala and runway show. The event showcased the work of five leading contemporary Indigenous designers: Jamie Okuma, Pamela Baker, Patricia Michaels, Jontay Kahm, and Lauren Good Day.

Santa Fe’s reputation as a Native fashion hub is well-established, with the annual Santa Fe Indian Market drawing over a thousand artists from more than 200 tribal nations. This weekend’s event, however, injected fresh energy into the city’s quieter months, spotlighting trailblazing artists who blend traditional crafts like beadwork, leatherwork, and textile arts with modern design.
articles.content_section_2:
Designers Blend Tradition With Contemporary Vision

Each designer presented five looks reflecting their unique visions. Jontay Kahm, a rising Plains Cree talent, crafted sculptural, feather-inspired garments from organza. Patricia Michaels showcased silk dresses with her signature brushstroke prints, inspired by natural elements. Pamela Baker paid homage to the Northwest Coast with elegant gowns featuring totemic designs, using traditional materials. Lauren Good Day brought vibrant ribbon skirts and accessories, emphasizing Native excellence and future generations.

articles.title: Fashion Trends You'll See in Spring 2026
articles.excerpt: Spring 2026 fashion trends highlight bold purple hues, polka dots, and playful styling, blending self-expression with comfort from runway to street style.
articles.content_section_1:
How Trends Shape Personal Style

Fashion trends may change season after season, but they continue to play a key role in helping individuals explore and define their personal style. For Spring 2026, the focus is on blending comfort, self-expression, and creativity with a touch of trend-driven inspiration.

Insights from trend forecasting platforms, resale reports, runway shows, and social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok have shaped this season’s standout looks. From high fashion runways to everyday street style, these influences highlight what consumers will be wearing throughout the year.

Ultimately, trends should serve as inspiration rather than strict rules—encouraging experimentation while staying true to individual style preferences.
articles.content_section_2:
All Purple Everything

Purple is emerging as one of the defining colors of 2026, dominating recent runway presentations with shades ranging from soft lavender to deep eggplant and rich burgundy. The color’s versatility makes it suitable for every season, from light, playful tones in spring and summer to deeper, more luxurious hues later in the year.

Celebrity influence has also played a role in elevating the trend, with standout appearances in bold, monochromatic purple looks. Styling options include mixing different textures such as sheer fabrics, sequins, and faux-feather details to create visually dynamic outfits.

Whether worn as a statement piece or as part of a layered ensemble, purple offers a fresh and expressive way to experiment with color.
articles.content_section_3:
Polka Dots Make a Comeback

Polka dots, a timeless and classic pattern, continue their resurgence into 2026 after gaining momentum in previous seasons. Seen across major runway collections, the print is being reimagined in modern silhouettes and transitional wardrobe pieces.

From slip dresses and knitwear to outerwear like coats and puffers, polka dots provide versatility for changing weather conditions. The pattern works especially well during the shift from winter to spring, offering both elegance and playfulness.

Paired with simple staples like knit sweaters or styled as bold statement pieces, polka dots remain a reliable and stylish choice for a variety of looks throughout the season.

articles.title: Mckenna Grace Revives Princess Pink Nails Trend at Oscars 2026
articles.excerpt: Mckenna Grace embraces the princess pink nails trend at the 2026 Oscars, pairing a monochromatic pink look with a timeless and elegant manicure.
articles.content_section_1:
Red Carpet Elegance in Pink

Mckenna Grace added a nice, modern touch to the Oscars 2026 red carpet by getting her nails done in the current style of princess pink. She wore a satin pink gown with a corset top and a full skirt that fell at the waist. A matching scarf was elegantly draped around her arms, which added to the overall look.

Her beauty was based on a single color scheme, which was clear in her pink blush, matching lips, and a polished hairstyle with her signature curtain bangs. For the final touch, Mckenna chose medium-length, almond-shaped nails painted a soft baby pink.
articles.content_section_2:
The Rise of Baby Pink Nails

Baby pink is one of the most popular nail colors right now, thanks to trends like Barbiecore, the coquette look, and minimalist beauty trends like the milk nails trend. Baby pink is a light-colored nail polish that can be worn for any occasion, from casual to formal.

The princess nails trend, is all about being simple and classy. It is based on royal nail art. This kind of nail art uses sheer colors that give nails a shiny look, like healthy nail beds.
articles.content_section_3:
A Timeless Beauty Staple

Princess pink nails are beautiful because they are always in style and never go out of style. The main reason for this is that the trend can be changed and everyone loves it.

Celebrities like Mckenna Grace wearing the trend on the red carpet show that it is here to stay and will be a favorite among people who like how chic baby pink nails look.

articles.title: Bebe x Betty Boop Collection Debuts in Pop Culture Style
articles.excerpt: Bebe marks its 50th anniversary with a Betty Boop capsule collection, blending vintage charm with modern glamour in a bold pop culture collaboration.
articles.content_section_1:
A Playful Fashion Collaboration Takes Center Stage

Fashion brand Bebe has officially stepped into the spotlight with its first-ever collaboration featuring the legendary cartoon icon Betty Boop. The debut Bebe x Betty Boop collection fuses two culturally powerful names, delivering a capsule designed for bold, fashion-forward women.

Known for its sultry, body-conscious aesthetic, Bebe reimagines Betty Boop through a more modern, elevated lens—transforming the vintage character into a symbol of contemporary glamour.
articles.content_section_2:
Reinventing an Icon for a New Generation

Betty Boop’s resurgence in pop culture—fueled in part by the success of Boop! The Musical on Broadway—has made her the perfect muse for a fresh fashion revival. The collection captures her playful, confident spirit while aligning it with Bebe’s signature sophistication.

Joey Gabbay, CEO of Bluestar Alliance, described the collaboration as a celebration of timeless style. By blending Betty’s classic iconography with modern design elements, the collection delivers a look that feels both nostalgic and undeniably current.
articles.content_section_3:
What’s Inside the Collection

The capsule includes a variety of pieces designed for both everyday wear and standout moments. From statement night-out outfits to relaxed denim, sleepwear, and wardrobe essentials, each piece incorporates bold prints, graphics, and motifs inspired by the world of Betty Boop.

The designs balance playful visuals with sleek silhouettes, making them versatile enough to transition from casual settings to evening looks.
articles.content_section_4:
A Milestone Moment for Bebe

The collection launched on March 24 exclusively online, marking a significant moment for Bebe as it celebrates its 50th anniversary. Founded in 1976, the brand has long been recognized for its chic, figure-flattering designs and its ability to blend day-to-night style seamlessly.

This collaboration is just one of several partnerships planned as part of the brand’s milestone year, highlighting its continued relevance at the intersection of fashion and pop culture.

articles.title: Alysa Liu’s Signature Spin on a Classic LBD at Music Awards
articles.excerpt: Alysa Liu brings her signature spin to a classic LBD at the iHeartRadio Music Awards, blending minimalism with bold, modern detailing.
articles.content_section_1:
A New Look for the Old LBD

Alysa Liu, a gold medalist at the Olympics, wore a simple but bold little black dress to the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards. The look was both stylish and elegant, with a halter neckline that dipped into a cutout bodice with satin bra cups. The pencil skirt was asymmetrical and went down to the ankles. Liu wore black pointed-toe pumps with the dress and kept her accessories to a minimum so that the edgy halter straps and pinup-style neckline could stand out.
articles.content_section_2:
Minimalist Glam Meets Fame on the Red Carpet

Liu's glam was simple but powerful. Her halo hair was straight with a slight bend at the ends. She wore a soft smoky eye, peach blush, lip gloss, and a short black manicure that matched her dress. Liu has been making waves on the red carpet since she won a gold medal at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Italy. She wore a fancy Louis Vuitton mini dress to the Vanity Fair Oscar Party and a sleek tuxedo-inspired outfit to the Elton John AIDS Foundation Oscar Party. Alysa Liu is becoming a style icon in her own right, even when she's not on the ice.
articles.image1_source: Alysa Liu at iHeartRadio Music Awards 2026

articles.title: KidSuper’s Colm Dillane Encourages Young Designers to Keep ‘Tripping Forward’
articles.excerpt: KidSuper founder Colm Dillane shares advice with emerging fashion talents, emphasizing resilience, collaboration, and embracing failure as a path to success.
articles.content_section_1:
A new wave of fashion talent is preparing to shape the industry, and KidSuper founder Colm Dillane is helping lead the charge. Hosting the 2026 Fashion Scholarship Fund (FSF) scholars at his studio, Dillane welcomed 160 students—including 60 Virgil Abloh™ “Post-Modern” Scholars—for a day of connection, inspiration, and mentorship ahead of the annual FSF gala.

At the event, Kuan Jackson of Clark Atlanta University was awarded the prestigious $25,000 Chairman’s Award. Other finalists included Rylee Funfsinn (Savannah College of Art and Design), Justin Le (University of Texas at Austin), and Madison-Jade Bishop (Drexel University). The Fashion Scholarship Fund remains committed to supporting aspiring professionals through its awards of more than $1.9 million in scholarships, mentorships, and connections.
articles.content_section_2: The designer also encouraged the students to make the most out of their current circumstances through relationship-building and collaboration. “You should get each other's numbers… try to collaborate as much as possible, and take as many risks as humanly possible,” he advised the students. Fashion is considered a privilege, and Dillane encouraged the students to stay motivated in the fashion industry despite its unpredictability by appreciating the opportunity to create.

articles.title: Portuguese Designers Bring Heritage Craftsmanship to the Global Fashion Stage
articles.excerpt: From Porto’s footwear legacy to Lisbon’s rising designers, Portugal is emerging as a global fashion force rooted in craftsmanship, culture, and innovation.
articles.content_section_1:
Located a few hours north of Lisbon, Porto has traditionally been at the core of Portugal’s footwear craftsmanship, with this industry quietly contributing to collections on many of the world’s biggest runways, such as those of Willy Chavarria, Kallmeyer, and Libertine, with support from APPICAPS, Portugal’s footwear federation. Now, a new wave of young designers from Lisbon is taking its place in the spotlight, bringing a new level of creativity to this rich heritage.

BÉHEN, Francisca Nabhino, and DRIONADREAM are just a few of the brands revolutionizing Portuguese fashion, with their collections demonstrating a unique balance of heritage and innovation, indicating a new and exciting trend in this country, which is quickly becoming a new hotbed of fashion.
articles.content_section_2:
In the Fall/Winter 2026 collection from BÉHEN, Joana Duarte's creations exemplify a celebration of both heritage and artisanal skill; this is particularly evident in her incorporation of hand-beaded embellishments crafted from genuine glass, which evoke the aesthetic of traditional Portuguese Algibeira embroidery.

Her designs are also a product of global culture, as seen in her collaboration with artists from around the world via projects such as Homelore, which utilizes traditional Gujarati mirrors for her design. This blend of local and global influences reflects a broader shift in Portuguese fashion. It's a movement centered on sustainable production methods and the power of storytelling through apparel.
articles.content_section_3: The other designer, Francisca Nabhino, has a different perspective on heritage in Portuguese fashion, though equally useful. The name of Francisca's collection, "LUCKY," is inspired by the Portuguese artist Almada Negreiros. It features colors, stars, and playful designs to address the theme of identity and liberation. The designer's work is a combination of intellectual and artistic inspirations, thus emphasizing the cultural heritage of Portugal.
articles.content_section_4: The group is also working closely with ModaLisboa in showing the best of Portugal’s fashion talent on a global stage. The industry in Portugal is not only about producing footwear for luxury fashion houses such as Loewe but also about supporting independent fashion designers and contemporary fashion brands.
articles.content_section_5: From the latest Fall/Winter collections shown by fashion designers in Portugal, the fashion industry in the country is characterized by its strong commitment to heritage, community, and sustainable production. With a strong sense of inspiration coming from its own cultural past and its open-mindedness to new ideas and concepts, Portugal is also creating a new and unique identity for itself in the global fashion industry.

articles.title: Exaggerated Lower Eyeliner Is the Groundbreaking Spring 2026 Makeup Trend
articles.excerpt: From Twiggy-inspired mod looks to bold graphic lines seen on Zendaya and Teyana Taylor, lower lashline eyeliner is making a dramatic comeback this spring.
articles.content_section_1:
If you haven't used eyeliner in a while, now is a great time to try it again. Bold eyeliner is making a big comeback after years of the "clean girl" look, especially on the lower lashline. Graphic, exaggerated strokes are the main focus right now. They are inspired by the famous 1960s style made famous by supermodel Twiggy, but they have been updated for 2026.

This comeback isn't totally surprising. Beauty trends have been hinting at a move toward more expressive makeup. For example, the resurgence of the smoky eye style, which was popularized around the end of 2025, and the statement liner style donned by celebrities like Jenna Ortega and Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu. This style, which was previously associated with grunge, is now looking sleek and mod.
articles.content_section_2:
A Look at the Lower Liner Trend

This trend has its roots in the 1960s, when celebrities like Twiggy, Diana Ross, and Cher made dramatic eyeliner on both the upper and lower lashlines popular. Their makeup often included winged liner and defined strokes on the lower lashes that looked like delicate, spidery lashes.

Makeup artist Kasey Spickard says that the revival of the 1980s is a sign that people want to be creative after years of being simple. The lower lashline is a new area to try out new things, and it lets you make designs that are fun, unexpected, and highly customizable that go against traditional beauty standards.
articles.content_section_3:
Where We've Seen It

At the Paris premiere of The Drama, Zendaya embraced the trend on the red carpet by wearing a hairstyle inspired by the past and precise, graphic eyeliner that went all the way around her eyes. The style, which was based on Sophia Loren's 1960s look, gave her eyes a more defined and larger appearance.

Teyana Taylor has also made bold lower liner a signature part of her awards season beauty looks. She often adds lighter colors to the waterline to make it look better. Other famous people who have used it are Giselle from Aespa, Rei Ami at the 2026 Academy Awards, and Olympic gold medalist Amber Glenn. Each of them put their own spin on it, from soft mod elegance to edgier, Y2K-inspired looks.
articles.content_section_4:
How to Get the Look

You need the right tools and a lot of patience to make a graphic lower liner look. Use a sharpened, creamy eyeliner pencil to start. This will make it easier to apply and set in place. Start by drawing a line along the top lashline, and then carefully draw a line along the bottom lashline to connect the two.

Kasey Spickard, a makeup artist, says to pick formulas that are easy to apply but won't smudge once they're set. Once you know the basics, try using negative space, bold waterline accents, or even colored liner to make it your own. A fine-tipped brush can help you make detailed designs, and a pointed cotton swab can clean up the edges for a perfect finish.

articles.title: Best Prom Makeup Ideas and Tips From the Pros to Try at Home
articles.excerpt: From smoky eyes to golden glam, discover expert-backed prom makeup ideas and tips to create the perfect look for your big night.
articles.content_section_1:
It's officially prom season, and that means it's time to get excited about planning every part of your look. It can be hard to pick the right outfit and do your hair and makeup, but it can also be a lot of fun. Prom is one of the biggest nights of the year, so your makeup should match your style and make you look good.

The best part is that you don't need to hire a professional artist to get a beautiful finish. You can get a perfect prom makeup look at home with things you probably already have if you follow the right advice and use your imagination.

Why Makeup Matters

Your makeup can change the whole look of your prom by bringing together your clothes, hair, and accessories. The most important thing is to pick a style that makes you feel good and brings out your best features, whether you like something soft and natural or bold and dramatic.

Instead of guessing what might work, you can get ideas from how celebrities look and get professional advice to help you get started. There are so many ways to use makeup to show who you are, from soft glam to old Hollywood style.

The Classic Smoky Eye
articles.content_section_2:
There is a reason why the smoky eye is still a prom favorite. It's bright, flexible, and looks great at formal events. A classic smoky eye is made up of deep black or charcoal colors blended into the crease and outer corners, with smudged liner along the lower lash line. This look is based on Alysa Liu's signature look.

Use a neutral face, like a soft bronzer and a nude lip, to balance out the dramatic eyes. This makes you look effortlessly stylish and put-together.

Glam that is soft and subtle
articles.content_section_3:
A softer smoky eye with gray tones can give you the same look as a bold black eye, but with a more subtle finish. A great example of this is Taylor Swift's subtle smoky look, which has a light wash of gray shadow that blends perfectly across the lids.

This is the best way to go if you want to look classy without going overboard. It's easy to make your own and goes well with a lot of different outfits and color schemes.

A Gold Pop
articles.content_section_4:
Gold accents can instantly make your makeup look bright and eye-catching. Gold tones add warmth and depth, whether you use them all over your lids or just as a highlight. You can get this look with metallic shadows or even your favorite highlighter, just like Leah Sava Jeffries.

Gold makeup goes well with many different outfits and gives you a bright, festive look that is perfect for prom night.

Beauty in Bronze
articles.content_section_5:
Bronze tones add depth and richness without being too strong. Zendaya's monochrome looks inspired this bronze makeup, which looks great on warm skin tones and can be used on the cheeks and lips for a smooth finish. This method gives you a glowing, sun-kissed look that is both modern and easy.

Creative Eyeliner Styles
articles.content_section_6:
If you want to stand out, trying out different eyeliners is a great way to do it. A classic cat-eye is always a good choice, but you can give it a new, modern look by doing something different, like extending the line from the lower lash line.

This look goes well with little makeup on the rest of your face, letting the liner stand out. Eyeliner can be changed to fit your style and make your prom look better, whether you like bold wings or a more subtle look.

articles.title: Dream Quotes from Icons to Spark Your Future
articles.excerpt: Discover dream quotes from iconic figures to spark your future, perfect for graduation speeches, captions, and celebrating your next chapter.
articles.content_section_1:
This is a significant milestone, a moment that is full of pride, reflection, and excitement for what the future will hold. Whether you are graduating from high school or college, this is a moment that is the end of something and a completely new beginning. It is a moment to celebrate the successes you have achieved, the struggles you have overcome, and the memories you have made along the way.

Finding the perfect words to express the emotions you are feeling during this milestone is not always easy. The perfect graduation quote will help you express what you are feeling, celebrate the journey you have been on, and give you the motivation you need for the future. Whether you’re trying to come up with the perfect Instagram post, the perfect words for a card, or the perfect words for a speech, the perfect quote will give you the power you need to make what you’re saying unforgettable.

With inspirational and thought-provoking words, funny and relatable sayings, the perfect graduation quote is a reflection on the many emotions you’re feeling during this milestone. Below, you’ll find a curated selection of the perfect graduation quotes, categorized by theme, to give you the confidence you need to move forward.
articles.content_section_2:
High school graduation quotes

  1. "Intelligence plus character—that is the goal of true education." —Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.
  2. "You can’t connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backward. You have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future." —Steve Jobs

  10. "You miss 100% of the shots you don't take." —Wayne Gretzky

College graduation quotes

  1. "The desire to reach the stars is ambitious. The desire to reach hearts is wise and most possible." —Maya Angelou

Graduation quotes for Instagram
