articles.title: Gen-Z Shopping: Separating Myth from Reality
articles.excerpt: Gen-Z is reshaping retail with strong spending power and evolving expectations, pushing brands to move beyond stereotypes to stay relevant.
articles.content_section_1:
Gen-Z is now one of the most important groups of consumers in the economy after the pandemic. McKinsey & Company says that this generation is already changing the way stores work. They make up about 40% of all consumers and have about $150 billion in buying power in the US alone. As more Gen-Z people enter the workforce and become financially independent, their impact is expected to grow even more. Bain & Company predicts that by 2035, they could make up nearly 40% of the global market for personal luxury goods.

Gen-Z's power goes beyond just how much money they have to spend. Deloitte's research shows that more than 60% of Gen-Z shoppers would rather buy from brands that share their values, especially when it comes to sustainability, diversity, and ethical practices. Also, an IBM report found that almost 70% of Gen-Z shoppers are willing to pay more for eco-friendly products. This shows that people are becoming more aware of what they buy.

Brands can now reach Gen-Z members through more channels than ever. Gen-Z members don't just want brands to sell products. They want brands to share their values, their beliefs, their identities, on social media platforms like TikTok, Instagram, Depop, ThredUp, etc. Fashion and retail are not only about consuming a lot for many members of Gen-Z; they are also about expressing themselves and fitting in with others.
articles.content_section_2:
Though they hold a lot of power, they are still not well understood. People do not give them enough credit, nor do they give them too much credit. Therefore, many brands rely on stereotypes rather than actual knowledge about Gen-Z. For example, though many brands believe that Gen-Z is a digital-first generation, studies done by the National Retail Federation indicate that a large percentage of Gen-Z members prefer to shop in a store, especially when they can switch between online and in-store shopping.

Another significant factor that affects their behavior is how sensitive they are to price. According to a survey conducted by Morning Consult in 2025, more than half of Gen-Z shoppers look for discounts or wait for a sale before buying a product. This is a clear indication of the level of financial stress that the population was going through as a result of the pandemic. Resale retail is a growing industry as a result of this; Poshmark and StockX are some of the platforms that Gen-Z is using.

This is particularly important since the economy is at a critical moment because of the Covid-19 pandemic. By understanding the key concepts regarding Gen-Z and how successful companies have handled the issue using their branding and marketing strategies, businesses can effectively position themselves to win the loyalty of this segment of the market. Ultimately, authentic brands with good value and shopping experiences across the board are the ones that will win the loyalty of Gen-Z.

articles.title: Capri Pants Trend 2026: The Retro Style Dominating Spring & Summer
articles.excerpt: Capri pants are trending in 2026, blending retro style with modern tailoring to redefine spring and summer fashion essentials.
articles.content_section_1:
Are you trying to find new clothes for the coming summer? Have you been wearing the same old jeans or tracks every day? Why not try on some capri pants? These retro-style, attractive and cropped pants will add an extra charm to even the most basic outfits and give you some new styling ideas for this spring and summer season. They have been quite popular as well as unpopular at times throughout history, but once you get a hang of wearing them, you’ll never regret wearing them!

The history of capri pants dates back to the 1950s and 1960s, when icons like Audrey Hepburn and Marilyn Monroe popularized them in movies and casual settings. Their influence transformed capris into a versatile staple—slim-fitting and cropped just below the knee or mid-calf. While the term “capri” specifically refers to this length, similar styles are also called pedal pushers or clam diggers. Stylist Julie Matos notes, “A true capri hits mid-calf. Longer styles are ankle pants, shorter ones are Bermuda shorts. The key is proportion, silhouette, and how you style them.”
articles.content_section_2:
Capris gained renewed popularity during the '90s and early 2000s, worn by stars like Jennifer Lopez and Christina Aguilera, and featured on popular TV shows such as Friends and Sabrina, the Teenage Witch. During that era, they appeared in relaxed, sporty, and casual styles, from classic to cargo.

Fast forward to today, capris have made a stylish comeback, partly fueled by our love for Y2K and '90s minimalism. As Matos explains, “Every revival reflects the current mood. Right now, fashion is leaning toward polish and tailored looks.” Street style icons like Bella Hadid frequently sport sharply tailored capris, and retailers are embracing the trend with more refined options.
articles.content_section_3:
However, the new version of capris in 2026 will be slim, stylish, and elegant. It will not be as relaxed as before but rather refined. It will suit anyone looking to have an updated fashion statement.

The fashion world has a myriad of styles in which one can wear capris. For instance, you may decide to use retro-themed designs such as those worn by Sabrina Carpenter or a vintage style like the one that Carolyn Bessette used. Capri pants will be great when worn during spring and summer since they show some skin while being modest enough. To look great in capris, one needs to find the right balance between proportions.

articles.title: Olivia Jade Launches O.piccola, Marking a New Chapter in Beauty
articles.excerpt: Content creator Olivia Jade is stepping into the beauty industry with her new cosmetics line, O.piccola, a brand centered around minimalist makeup and personal reinvention.
articles.content_section_1:
A Carefully Curated New Beginning

On a late-April afternoon, Olivia Jade sits in a hotel opposite Madison Square Park. Her outfit is simple—a white tank top paired with jeans—except for a striking choker featuring four glittering strands and a Victorian-style pendant at the center. Her face draws the focus: minimal makeup emphasizing her eyes, radiant skin enhanced with a warm, sun-kissed bronzer. It’s a thoughtfully curated look, especially fitting for someone launching a beauty brand where bronzer and highlighter are key products.

After building a substantial following as a content creator online, Olivia Jade, daughter of actress Lori Loughlin and fashion designer Mossimo Giannulli, is venturing into a new chapter—launching her own cosmetics line, O.piccola. The brand’s name, Italian for “little,” reflects her “personal beauty philosophy that a little goes a long way,” according to the press release. The phrase “a little bit,” which she frequently uses in her makeup tutorials—whether referring to bronzer or concealer—serves as a central theme.
articles.content_section_2:
From Influencer to Beauty Founder

Jade isn’t positioning herself as a makeup expert. She describes her interest in cosmetics as natural and guided by tutorials on her YouTube channel, complemented by her father’s business acumen. Of course, her path hasn’t been without controversy; she’s faced public scrutiny and some missteps along the way. Fans may have noticed she’s been hinting at this launch for years, delayed by unforeseen challenges.

Jade, however, wants to be given a second chance. “I just really want people to know it's not a brand that I just wanted to slap my name on and do quick turnaround time and make some good cash,” she told. “I want people to know that it was a very long process of me trying to perfect a formula that's not on the market.”

articles.title: Freckles Emerge as a Standout Beauty Trend at Grammys 2026
articles.excerpt: Celebrities embraced natural freckles at the Grammys 2026, signaling a shift toward minimal makeup and authentic beauty.
articles.content_section_1:
A Subtle Yet Powerful Red Carpet Trend

A subtle yet powerful trend emerged on the red carpet at the 2026 Grammys: freckles. Initially, it was easy to overlook, but a closer examination of the evening's attendees showed a surprising embrace of natural skin texture. Many celebrities, it turned out, were celebrating their freckles, even choosing to let them shine on their faces.

This change is part of a bigger trend in beauty that values being real and unique. It means moving away from heavy coverage and toward more natural, skin-forward makeup looks.
articles.content_section_2:
Minimal Makeup, Maximum Impact

To show off their freckles, some artists chose soft glam and barely-there foundation. Billie Eilish wore a simple look with soft winged eyeliner, blurred lips, and a hint of warm blush that made her natural beauty stand out.

Sabrina Carpenter did something similar, using a dark pink lip, soft blush, and very little eye makeup so that her freckles could still be seen. Olivia Dean, on the other hand, used a bold lip to contrast her freckles, making a look that was both striking and balanced.

Lola Young and other artists have used freckles in their signature looks for a long time. Daniela and Lara, who are members of KATSEYE, also embraced the trend by not covering up their natural beauty marks.
articles.content_section_3:
The Rise of Natural Beauty

The trend for freckles is growing, just like the trends for glass skin and cloud skin before it. However, this time the focus is on self-expression. Freckles, whether real or fake, are now a big part of what makes modern beauty beautiful.

Earlier appearances, like Mckenna Grace at the Critics Choice Awards, showed that this trend was becoming more popular. Freckles will continue to be a big beauty trend in 2026, as more and more famous people wear them on the red carpet.

articles.title: Stand Oil Everyday Purses Turn It-Bags via Celebrities
articles.excerpt: Stand Oil’s everyday purses gain global traction as celebrities drive their rise into must-have It-bags.
articles.content_section_1:
If you are an avid shopper and have recently looked for a new bag, then you have probably heard of Stand Oil. This Korean brand has been slowly gaining popularity due to their trendy yet timeless designs that can easily blend in with any style. Not to mention, the stylish appeal of the brand has also garnered the attention of the world’s leading celebrities. In addition, the brand has also reached the height of TikTok popularity. Furthermore, the current popularity of the brand as an ‘it-bag’ creator has also reached new heights with the brand’s first-ever collaboration with none other than LE SSERAFIM’s Huh Yunjin.

The brand’s name is inspired by ‘stand oil,’ a thick and honey-colored linseed oil used in painting to achieve smooth, glossy, and long-lasting finishes. This inspires the brand to produce products that not only have current popularity but also stand the test of time. This is further explained by Yun Jo, the head of brand communications, stating, “In art, stand oil is used to achieve the finish, and it gets deeper and purer with time. We thought it would be great if our products could have that kind of quality.”
articles.content_section_2: She adds, “Rather than chasing fleeting trends, we aim to design bags that remain relevant and sophisticated over the years. When we launched the brand, we saw a gap in the market for accessories that combine thoughtful design, high quality, and affordable prices, all while feeling emotionally connected to daily life. Our mission has always been to create pieces that seamlessly fit into everyday routines while showcasing a distinctive yet understated style.”
articles.content_section_3:
Huh Yunjin’s connection to Stand Oil deepened during her first trip to the US with LE SSERAFIM. She was spotted carrying her Stand Oil bag decorated with ribbons, a small detail that inspired the collaboration. The collection features the mini Breezy bag, a smaller version of the brand’s popular style, in a “misty white” vegan leather shade, along with the Jenaissante charm set and scarf—embracing the bag charm trend.

Yunjin was initially drawn to the brand’s aesthetic. “In 2023, I bought a Stand Oil bag and styled it with a ribbon. I shared it on social media, and it unexpectedly went viral. Since then, I’ve built a strong relationship with Stand Oil, and that bag holds special meaning for me. When designing this piece, I kept that memory in mind and drew inspiration from how I styled it back then,” she shared.

articles.title: Spring Nail Ideas That Will Define 2026: Sage Green, Polka Dots & More
articles.excerpt: From glossy popsicle hues to sage green tones and playful polka dots, 2026’s spring nail trends blend minimal chic with bold, expressive designs.
articles.content_section_1:
The Rise of Nails as a Style Statement

The manicure is officially the smallest and most powerful accessory in fashion in 2026. No longer an afterthought, nail color, shape, and design are at the forefront of defining personal style. In fact, they can serve as the final touch that brings an entire look together.

Nail art has come a long way thanks to celebrity influences, social media crazes, and runway looks. From baby pink and midnight blue to milky sheens, chrome finishes, and jelly textures, the manicure is about individuality.

1. Popsicle Nails: Juicy Layers Take Over
articles.content_section_2:
This season, the nail art will be a delicious new look. Popsicle nail art, where the nail polish has a transparent look with colorful layers, will be the most popular. Think of cherry red, tangerine orange, watermelon pink, and electric raspberry all mixed together to make shiny, glass-like finishes.

The trend is very much about layering and abstract application, which gives the look of depth and makes it look almost edible. Tom Bachik, a famous nail artist, has already praised the style. Stars like Selena Gomez and Anna Cathcart have also embraced the juicy look on the red carpet.

2. Sage Green: The New Neutral
articles.content_section_3:
Dark greens are out; soft, herby sage tones are in for 2026. These soft, chalky greens give spring manicures a fresh, earthy look that is just the right mix of subtle and bold.

3. Polka Dot Nails: Playful Meets Classic
articles.content_section_4:
Polka dots are coming back in a big way, combining old-fashioned charm with new ideas. There are endless combinations, from classic black-and-white to bold ones like brown bases with pastel or neon dots.

Mixing patterns and layering designs, which artists like Queenie Nguyen made popular, gives this classic look a modern, stylish twist.

4. Cool Blue Nails: A Year-Round Favorite
articles.content_section_5:
Finishes like chrome and cat-eye effects make the look even better, so blue nails are good for any time of year and any event. Predictions like Pinterest Predicts have even made the trend last longer.

5. Butter Yellow: Soft Sunshine Style
articles.content_section_6:
Butter yellow nails are back, and they add a warm, creamy glow to manicures in the spring and summer. This happy but classy color goes well with both simple styles and fun nail art.

Butter yellow is a bright new color that feels both modern and classic, whether it's used with shiny finishes, matte textures, or small details like tiny dots.

articles.title: Easy, Breezy & Elegant: Paris Fashion Week Wearable Trends
articles.excerpt: Paris Fashion Week showcased a blend of intricate details and effortless styling, with scarves, lace, and florals defining the season’s easy-to-wear elegance.
articles.content_section_1:
Effortless Style Takes Center Stage

Paris Fashion Week, which concluded on Tuesday, offered a fresh blend of craftsmanship and effortless style. Both on the runway and on the streets, fashion took a strong stance on effortless styles, which can be worn with ease.

The overall feel of the event was light, playful, and palatable, offering a balance of craftsmanship and effortless styles that can be worn by everyone, beyond the realm of fashion.
articles.content_section_2:
Scarves, Lace, and Florals Dominate

One of the most prominent accessories of the season was graphic silk scarves, which were spotted on all runways and streets. These scarves were worn in various styles, including on the head, around the neck, on the shoulder, on the bag, or around the waist, making them a defining fashion statement of the season. Major fashion labels such as Loewe and Celine featured graphic scarves, which came in various styles, including bright primary colors, a color scheme that was also adopted by other labels such as Lacoste.

On the other hand, lace was used to add a touch of softness and lightness to collections by Chloé, Cecilie Bahnsen, and Off-White, whereas florals were spotted on almost all runways and streets.

articles.title: Gen Z’s Love for Astrology Is Shaping Jewelry Trends
articles.excerpt: From zodiac pendants to celestial motifs, Gen Z’s fascination with astrology is driving a surge in personalized, symbolic jewelry.
articles.content_section_1:
Gen Z uses astrology as a way to express themselves, and questions about sun signs, moon signs, and zodiac identities are now part of everyday conversation. People often include their astrological signs in their bios or in their posts on social media in order to quickly and easily describe their personalities. Laurent François, a creative consultant, states that "Astrology is an extremely significant part of digital culture." This is because millions of people utilize apps that allow them to read their horoscopes, such as Co-Star or #witchtok on TikTok.

This desire for digital culture has easily transitioned into the real world, especially in relation to fashion or accessories. Zodiac-themed jewelry is a strong way for people to express their identities by wearing something that is significant to them in a fashionable manner.
articles.content_section_2:
High-end jewelry stores are capitalizing on this growing need. Van Cleef & Arpels is known for its Alhambra design but is now releasing new pieces in its Zodiaque collection. This collection includes zodiac-themed medals, pendants, or bracelets. The new pieces in this collection are more affordable. The collection was first thought of in the early 1900s and became popular in the 1970s. Its revival shows that people are once again interested in astrological symbols.

Both retailers and designers are doing well in this category. Mytheresa and other platforms say that zodiac and celestial pieces are some of their best-selling fine jewelry designs. Brands like 886 by The Royal Mint and Goossens have also had success with astrology-themed collections. The appeal comes from the balance between personalization and classic style. This gives customers a way to show off their own style while still looking classy and being able to wear it with anything.

articles.title: White Skinny Jeans Are Back—Here Are the Shoes That Make Them Chic
articles.excerpt: White skinny jeans are making a stylish comeback for spring 2026, but the secret to pulling them off lies in pairing them with the right shoes.
articles.content_section_1:
White skinny jeans are making an unexpected comeback for Spring 2026, proving, once again, that fashion loves a comeback. White skinny jeans, which were once thought to be a tricky or uncool fashion option, are making a comeback with a new twist. The twist, however, is not in the jeans themselves but in how they are styled, particularly with what kind of shoe.

The right shoe can instantly transform white skinny jeans from stiff to chic, from uncool to fashionable. These jeans, with their simple aesthetic and relaxed fit, don't require a lot of embellishment. A well-chosen pair of shoes is all that's needed to add some personality and complete the outfit.
articles.content_section_2:
Soft Loafers

Soft loafers can make for an effortless, chic look with white skinny jeans. The relaxed design of the shoe can tone down the formality of the white skinny jeans, making for a look that is both chic and polished. The soft finish of the shoe, whether in leather or suede, can make for a look that is both relaxed and informal, making it suitable for everyday wear.
articles.content_section_3:
Mesh ballet flats

Mesh ballet flats provide a sense of lightness that works well with the structured denim. The semi-sheer look adds a touch of mystery without stealing the show. Those white skinny jeans? They've got a contemporary edge. Paired with simple items like a black blouse and understated jewelry, the whole thing comes together in a polished, stylish way.
articles.content_section_4:
Kitten-heel thong sandals

Kitten-heel thong sandals provide a sense of elegance with ease. Black kitten-heel thong sandals make for a striking contrast with white skinny jeans. This outfit makes for a seamless transition from casual daytime events to more formal events. This is because understated styles have the most impact. When paired with a basic T-shirt and casual outerwear, the outfit is chic.

articles.title: Ballet Leotards Will Be Spring 2026’s Biggest Trend, Thanks to Rosalía
articles.excerpt: Rosalía’s off-duty ballerina style is bringing leotards back into everyday fashion, inspiring a fresh balletcore trend for spring 2026.
articles.content_section_1:
Rosalía has been wearing ballet-inspired clothes for a long time, but her recent street style moment in Madrid is making ballet leotards a big trend for spring 2026. The artist was seen leaving a restaurant before her concerts at the Movistar Arena. She wore a simple leotard with casual layers, showing that balletcore is no longer just for the stage.

Her connection to dance, which was clear in her performances on the Lux tour, has helped bring back the romantic look of ballet into mainstream fashion. Rosalía continues to blend performance wear with everyday style, no matter if she's on pointe in a tutu or not working in a simple leotard.
articles.content_section_2:
This style is characterized by a nostalgic, yet challenging, style of dressing like a ballerina outside of work. This style typically includes a combination of dancewear such as a leotard, leg warmers, and a wrap layer, along with other clothing to create a unique and, at times, bizarre look. Rosalía was able to replicate this style quite easily by wearing a leotard, sweat pants, a sweatshirt, an oversized coat, and Ugg boots. She completed her look with a Dior Book tote.

This way of styling shows that leotards can be used for more than just performances; they can also be worn every day.
articles.content_section_3:
Rosalía's outfit also shows that people are starting to think of dancewear as everyday clothing. Transforming a leotard into a standout outfit is simple. Pair it with a miniskirt and high boots for a night on the town, or opt for jeans and sneakers for a more laid-back vibe. The secret is to mix structured, form-fitting shapes with looser, everyday clothes.

As balletcore changes, Rosalía's impact is making fashion lovers look through their closets and try on things that were only meant for the studio. This is why the simple leotard is going to be a big trend this season.

articles.title: Cobalt Blue Takes Over as Fashion’s New ‘It’ Color for 2026
articles.excerpt: From runways to red carpets, cobalt blue is replacing softer hues as the bold, dominant color trend of 2026.
articles.content_section_1:
The color palette of fashion is once again undergoing a transformation, and this time, the color that is at the forefront is none other than cobalt blue. While earlier, this vibrant color was not as prominent as other fun colors such as butter yellow, Barbie pink, and brat green, it is now set to be the defining color of 2026.

While the transformation has not been overnight, fashion houses such as Balenciaga, Chloé, Valentino, and Schiaparelli were some of the first to incorporate cobalt blue prominently in their spring 2025 fashion shows, thus giving birth to a revolutionary trend in the world of fashion.
articles.content_section_2:
The trend was at its peak in spring 2026, with renowned fashion brands such as Victoria Beckham, Gucci, Luar, Tom Ford, and Valentino using cobalt blue in their respective fashion shows, from dominant to subtle usage of the color in their clothing. The trend continued its stronghold into the fall 2026 season. Notable names like Chanel, Givenchy, Mugler, Stella McCartney, and Chloé showcased the bold hue in their fashion presentations.

Away from the runways, celebrities have also been integral in popularizing the fashion trend. At the Series Mania Festival in Lille, Chase Infiniti wore an eye-catching Louis Vuitton gown. Jessie Buckley and Wunmi Mosaku also wore cobalt blue as they received BAFTA awards. Dua Lipa also wore the fashion color as she attended the Elton John AIDS Foundation Oscars Viewing Party in a custom Gucci ensemble.
articles.content_section_3:
The fashion trend is also gaining traction through street fashion and film promotions. Bella Hadid wore the fashion color in a Saint Laurent resort ensemble, while Amelia Gray wore a Gucci faux fur ensemble in the same color. Dakota Johnson wore an eye-catching Gucci ensemble at the Zurich Film Festival, while Greta Lee and Alexander Skarsgård wore the fashion color in tailored ensembles during promotional events.

Through its blending with both casual and sophisticated fashion statements, cobalt blue is demonstrating its flexibility in different styles and environments.

From the runways to the red carpets, to the streets, cobalt blue is redefining the current trends in modern color. Its boldness, flexibility, and appeal guarantee its place as the next big thing in color, replacing the softer shades with this bright and beautiful color.

articles.title: Skin-Tone Hair Is Spring 2026’s Most Subtle Beauty Trend
articles.excerpt: From soft blondes to rich brunettes, skin-tone hair is redefining low-contrast beauty with shades tailored to your complexion.
articles.content_section_1:
In a world where beauty trends are becoming more and more contrasting in nature, a new trend has emerged that takes a different approach: skin tone hair. This trend has been popularized on social media and has been adopted by celebrity hairstylists everywhere. It involves dyeing your hair the same tone as your skin, as close as possible. This creates a seamless look, giving you a glowing effect.

According to celebrity hairstylist SherriAnn Cole, this look produces a “skin-enhancing effect that naturally brightens the face.” This look does not involve contrasts; instead, it’s taking a subtle approach, giving you a polished look without looking too done-up, perfect for spring 2026.
articles.content_section_2:
Undertones are an essential factor in getting the perfect skin-tone hair look. Experts stress that it is very important to match your hair tone with your skin undertones, whether it is warm or cool. According to hairstylist Jacob Schwartz, if you do not consider undertones, your hair will look like it does not belong with your skin. Instead, it is very important to choose your hair shades, such as warm caramel shades for golden skin, champagne blonde shades for lighter skin, and rich shades for darker skin.

While your hair perfectly complements your skin, it is very important to maintain contrast with your other features. According to both Cole and Schwartz, it is very important to make your eyebrows darker than your hair to avoid flatness. The final look should be balanced, with a low contrast look that is cohesive yet individualistic. As both Margot Robbie and Zendaya are already sporting this look, skin-tone hair is set to become one of the most refined beauty trends of the season.

articles.title: Celebrity-Approved Lob Haircuts for a Fresh 2026 Refresh
articles.excerpt: Discover celebrity-approved lob haircuts for 2026, from sleek styles to soft waves, perfect for a fresh and stylish hair refresh.
articles.content_section_1:
The Lob: The Perfect In-Between Haircut

This spring, bobs are taking center stage—but if you’re not quite ready for a dramatic chop, the long bob (or “lob”) is the perfect compromise. Cutting between the chin and collarbone, the lob gives a fresh, modern look without sacrificing too much length.

Celebrities like Halle Berry and Selena Gomez have embraced shorter styles, but the lob remains a go-to for those wanting versatility. Hairstylist María Baras describes it as ideal for anyone seeking change without going too short. Whether worn blunt and structured like Victoria Beckham’s iconic early-2000s cut or soft and fluid like Kim Kardashian’s collarbone-grazing “clavi-cut,” the lob adapts effortlessly to personal style.

The reason for its universal appeal is the fact that it suits a variety of face shapes, styles with various partings and bangs, and is still long enough to allow for various hairstyles. Whether sleek, messy, or layered for movement, the lob is still a timeless option with the seal of approval from various celebrities for a subtle yet impactful look.

articles.title: Belfast’s Linen Legacy: Reviving an Old Industry to Shape the City’s New Identity
articles.excerpt: Belfast is rediscovering its historic linen industry, blending heritage, design, and sustainability to craft a new cultural identity.
articles.content_section_1:
In Belfast’s Cathedral Quarter, the boutique Kindred of Ireland is showcasing artfully oversized linen blouses and tweed jackets, demonstrating how the city is embracing its historic textile roots. Half a century after the Troubles, linen—a fibre that once made Belfast the so-called Linenopolis—is reemerging as a symbol of renewal and identity. Amy Anderson, designer at Kindred of Ireland, notes that linen holds deep significance for locals, connecting generations to the city’s past while supporting a modern, architectural aesthetic.

The revival of linen is supported by a diverse group consisting of fashion designer Sarah Burton, the Prince and Princess of Wales, and Charlie Mallon, who is growing and working with flax in a regenerative way on the heritage farm he owns. This is done by reviving old equipment and using techniques such as beetling to maintain the integrity of the fiber, which will give linen its original properties, opposing the modern and mass-produced version of the material that is often processed in China.
articles.content_section_2: Apart from manufacturing, the resurgence of linen in Belfast also involves culture and fashion. Sarah Burton used Belfast’s factories as a source of inspiration for designing apparel that was showcased on the Paris catwalk, while the interest of the Prince and Princess of Wales in linen shows its international significance. The ‘Ashes to Fashion’ exhibition curated by the Ulster Museum is another instance of Belfast’s history of fashion and revival, which includes clothing from 18th-century silks to contemporary garments designed by Irish fashion designers. From the point of view of business, Kindred of Ireland plans to establish pop-up shops to promote its business further.

articles.title: Zendaya’s Bridal Makeup Gets a Spring Eyeshadow Makeover
articles.excerpt: Zendaya’s bridal makeup gets a spring-inspired makeover with bold eyeshadow, redefining modern bridal beauty trends.
articles.content_section_1:
Zendaya and her team have given classic wedding traditions a new look through fashion and beauty during the Drama press tour. The actress has put a playful spin on wedding traditions, recycling her 2015 Oscars gown as her "something old." She's also incorporated borrowed and blue items into her ensemble. Her beauty looks have also changed, showing off a glowing, polished style that goes well with her method of dressing.

Zendaya showed off a unique look at the premiere after-party. She wore metallic blue eyeshadow that was darker in the middle and turned into a smoky cat-eye. The look had a more laid-back, post-ceremony feel thanks to the warm coral contour, silver-highlighted glow, and glossy nude-pink lip. Her slicked-back bixie cut with a wet finish added to the polished but easy-going look, which went perfectly with her sheer midnight blue gown.
articles.content_section_2:
This brave beauty look is a reflection of a broader movement within wedding fashion, in which brides become increasingly comfortable with changing both their outfits and makeup multiple times throughout their weddings. Today’s bridal makeup trends do not confine themselves to classic minimalism but venture into vibrant hues, dramatic makeup, and unique hairstyles.

More specifically, blue eyeshadow is an iconic trend of the current season, and it illustrates just how people have been gravitating towards maximalism. The look is now both multifunctional and attention-grabbing, especially considering how the eyeshadows can be diffused or pearlescent. Zendaya's makeup artists, such as Ernesto Casillas and Ursula Stephen, are currently developing it, combining bold accents and radiant skin. This means that there will be even more innovative beauty moments on the press tour.

articles.title: Dua Lipa Makeup Artist’s Fuller Lips Trick: Micro-Lining Explained
articles.excerpt: Dua Lipa’s makeup artist shares a micro-lining technique for fuller-looking lips, offering a natural, minimal approach to enhancing lip shape in 2026.
articles.content_section_1:
Katie Jane Hughes, makeup artist to the likes of Dua Lipa, Hailey Bieber, and Lily Allen, reveals a groundbreaking trick that will allow you to add volume to your lips while maintaining that blurry look. It is a minimalist hack that provides the look of over-lined lips without looking overly fake, and all you need to do is make some marks with a lip liner and fluffy brush.

As explained by Hughes, micro-lining involves creating the impression of plumper lips through focusing on certain parts of your mouth. One important aspect in carrying out the technique successfully is choosing the perfect lip liner shade. In her demonstration, Katie uses the darkest shade from the KJH.Brand’s Prism Lite Lip Crayon line. Nevertheless, for regular use, a color only one shade darker than your skin is sufficient.

The technique involves making four precise lines: two at the corners of the mouth to create a subtle recess, and two on the cupid's bow and the center of the bottom lip to enhance fullness. These small, overlined marks are then blended with a fluffy makeup brush. "This creates the soft, blurred finish without removing the product," Hughes notes. The amount of blending can be adjusted based on personal preference—less blending for more definition, and more blending for a softer effect.

For that perfect finish, one can add a hint of lip gloss as well, which will make the lips appear fuller and more defined. With this technique, any individual is able to get fuller lips effortlessly.

articles.title: Jenna Ortega Stuns in Daring Glass Mosaic Dress on Late Show Appearance
articles.excerpt: Jenna Ortega turned heads in a striking Oscar de la Renta glass mosaic dress while sharing behind-the-scenes stories about working with Lady Gaga on Wednesday.
articles.content_section_1:
Jenna Ortega is known for making an entrance, and on this occasion, she literally sparkled on The Late Show With Stephen Colbert. The actress made waves in an jaw-dropping strapless red gown designed by Oscar de la Renta, made exclusively from glass shards. The gown was inspired by the beauty of stained glass and can be considered nothing less than a masterpiece.

Although the look was visually stunning, there was a sharp edge to this look. Ortega candidly admitted that the gown was not exactly the most comfortable choice for her and joked that on occasion, glass shards poked her when she sat down. “I feel uncomfortable and extremely comfortable at the same time,” she quipped, and that is exactly what this look was all about.
articles.content_section_2:
Apart from the stunning outfit, Ortega also gave fans a look at what it was like to work with Lady Gaga for the upcoming season of Wednesday. Ortega explained that the whole atmosphere changed the moment the pop star arrived on the set.

The regular filming location suddenly turned into a full-scale upgrade of the entire production, including the arrival of multiple trucks, a special walkway for the pop star, and even a change to the cast’s facilities. Ortega joked about how the regular setup of hers was replaced with luxury, including a barber’s chair and better refreshments, all to accommodate the global superstar.
articles.content_section_3:
Despite the glamour and luxury surrounding the pop star, Ortega explained how, behind the scenes, Lady Gaga was anything but intimidating. Ortega explained how the pop star was actually quite sweet, humble, and down-to-earth, despite being incredibly talented.

The look, of course, was not only a testament to Ortega’s growing status as a star but also a perfect blend of style, humor, and charm, proving that at times, the most memorable looks come with a little bit of discomfort and a whole lot of personality.
articles.image2_source: The Late Show With Stephen Colbert

articles.title: Stars Bring Glamour to the Oscars 2026 Red Carpet
articles.excerpt: From vibrant spring hues to timeless black and white ensembles, celebrities delivered standout fashion moments at the 98th Academy Awards.
articles.content_section_1:
The 98th Academy Awards brought together some of the biggest stars in Hollywood. The red carpet was a mix of bright colors and classic style. This year's fashion had a lot of spring-like colors, which made the event feel new and exciting.

Jessie Buckley made a statement in a regal ensemble, featuring a red, off-the-shoulder bodice paired with a sweeping pink ball skirt, courtesy of Chanel. Chase Infiniti, on the other hand, looked stunning in a lilac sleeveless gown by Louis Vuitton. The fitted bodice and cascading ruffles made a dramatic train.

Director Spike Lee, wore a bright purple hat and bow tie that added a splash of color to his outfit. He has worn this color before as a tribute to Prince.
articles.content_section_2:
Black and white ensembles made a strong showing on the red carpet, proving their enduring appeal. Rose Byrne, up for an award for her performance in If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, certainly understood the assignment, opting for a strapless black Dior gown. The dress was subtly enhanced with delicate white floral accents.

Emma Stone,dazzled in a floor-length, shimmering white gown from Louis Vuitton. The dress featured graceful cap sleeves and a streamlined silhouette. She was nominated for Bugonia.
articles.content_section_3:
Teyana Taylor, who stood out all awards season, wore a black and white feathered gown by Chanel that was another memorable look. The sleeveless style had a sheer panel that gave the classic color scheme a modern twist.

The Oscars 2026 red carpet was a mix of bold experimentation and classic style. Celebrities wore bright seasonal colors and elegant monochrome looks.

articles.title: Zendaya’s Bixie Cut Steals the Spotlight at Oscars 2026
articles.excerpt: Zendaya showcases the bixie cut at the Oscars 2026, cementing the hybrid hairstyle as one of the biggest hair trends to watch this year.
articles.content_section_1:
When Zendaya showed off her version of the bixie cut at the 2026 Oscars, she started a new beauty trend. The hairstyle is already getting a lot of attention, and after the actor showed off a few different ways to wear it in just one week, it is now being called one of the year's most important hair trends.

A pixie and a bob make up the bixie cut. It has short layers and a soft, textured look. It has the shape of a bob and the easy, slightly messy look of a pixie, which makes it both trendy and useful.
articles.content_section_2:
Zendaya wore her bixie cut straight with a side part and soft bangs to the Oscars. It looked smooth and shiny. She put some of her hair behind her ears and flicked the ends out at the back to show off the cut's layers. She still looked classy on the red carpet.

The bixie demonstrated its versatility, proving it could be both elegant and playful.
articles.content_section_3:
At ESSENCE Black Women in Hollywood Awards 2026, held earlier this week, Zendaya debuted a fresh style, curling her bixie cut into soft, elegant waves. Her bangs were curled into soft ringlets that framed her face and made it look romantic and textured.

The bixie cut is a new twist on popular layered hairstyles like wolf cuts and modern bobs. It has a nice balance of structure and movement, so it's a stylish and easy-to-care-for choice for 2026.

articles.title: Zendaya Stuns in Louis Vuitton at The Drama Paris Premiere
articles.excerpt: Zendaya stuns at The Drama Paris premiere in a custom Louis Vuitton gown, featuring a bridal-inspired look with a bold backless silhouette.
articles.content_section_1:
At the Paris premiere of The Drama, Zendaya had one of the most talked-about red carpet moments of the year. She wore a custom white Louis Vuitton gown that was both simple and dramatic, thanks to Law Roach's styling. The front of the dress had a sleek column shape, and the back had a dramatic open design with a big black bow and a flowing train.

The look was also described as a "wedding cake topper" due to the mix of sculptural elegance and bridal inspiration. The outfit was both modern and memorable due to the clean white fabric and the bold use of black accents.
articles.content_section_2:
Zendaya is continuing to employ her "method dressing" look during the movie's press tour. The drama film, which stars Robert Pattinson, is about a couple who is going through a crazy wedding week.

Her Paris look was the "something new" part of a traditional wedding theme, following earlier looks that were inspired by other wedding traditions. This consistent styling approach has made her red carpet choices more interesting and full of stories.
articles.content_section_3:
Zendaya finished off the look with black pumps that matched the bow detail and high-end jewelry diamonds from David Morris. Her hair and makeup stayed clean and up-to-date, which kept the dress the main focus.

The bridal-inspired look has also sparked rumors about her personal life, especially her relationship with Tom Holland, since she has been seen wearing a gold ring on her ring finger. Even though nothing has been confirmed, the fashion story keeps people talking. Overall, the look made Zendaya look like one of Hollywood's most powerful and strategic style icons.

articles.title: Taylor Swift Wins Artist of the Year at 2026 iHeartRadio Awards
articles.excerpt: Taylor Swift wins Artist of the Year at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, reflecting on her journey and encouraging fans to follow their passions.
articles.content_section_1:
Swift Honored at the iHeartRadio Music Awards

Taylor Swift won Artist of the Year at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards. Olympic gold medalist Alysa Liu gave the award at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles on March 26. Swift, who is 36 years old, won seven awards in all. In her acceptance speech, she talked about how important it is to have passion, practice, and perseverance when trying to improve at something. She praised Liu for her hard work and the happiness she brings to the world, connecting it to the goals of the people in the audience.
articles.content_section_2:
Advice to Fans: Nurture Your Hobby

In her touching speech, Swift talked about her childhood and how writing songs went from being a hobby to being the most important thing in her life. She stressed how important it is to spend time learning, practicing, and making mistakes on your own before asking for validation from others. Swift told her fans to work on their own crafts away from the stress of social media. She said, "Give yourself time to make mistakes, give yourself time to hone your craft." She ended by thanking the audience for letting her turn her hobby into a job and wishing them success in their creative work.
articles.content_section_3:
Recognizing Other Nominees

Nominees for Artist of the Year included Bad Bunny, Benson Boone, Chris Brown, Jelly Roll, Kendrick Lamar, Lady Gaga, Morgan Wallen, Sabrina Carpenter, and Tate McRae, highlighting the competitive field of talented artists in 2026.
articles.image1_source: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift at the iHeartRadio Music Awards in Los Angeles on March 26, 2026. Christopher Polk/Billboard via Getty

articles.title: Miley Cyrus Moves Beyond Hannah Montana at iHeartRadio 2026
articles.excerpt: Miley Cyrus debuts a bold new style at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards, moving beyond Hannah Montana nostalgia while honoring its impact.
articles.content_section_1:
Miley Cyrus’ appearance at the 2026 iHeartRadio Music Awards is quite different from her latest Hannah Montana-inspired outfits. While the singer chose to don a modern style with her classy leather coat and cigarette pants with black pumps, she has moved away from the nostalgic look and has opted for something more modern and stylish.

Cyrus’ style is changing, and her appearance with her classy jewelry and her signature blonde hair with a grungy smoky eye is reflective of the fact that she is more mature and confident now. Her appearance is quite different from her latest appearance at her anniversary party, where she donned fun outfits inspired by her Disney Channel days.
articles.content_section_2:
The iHeartRadio Music Awards took place at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, where the singer won the prestigious Innovator Award for her impact on the music and entertainment industries over the past 20 years. This award is a testament to the fact that the singer has come a long way from her days on the Disney Channel and is now a renowned pop star known for her changing style.

In her acceptance speech, Cyrus talked about how her career has changed over time and how important her early years in the spotlight were. She thanked the journey that shaped her art.
articles.content_section_3:
Cyrus said that Hannah Montana still has an impact on her life and career, even though her fashion choice was a step in the right direction. She said that the character helped her find confidence and creative freedom, and that sometimes taking on a role can help you find your true self.

This happens during a big week for Cyrus, right after the release of the Hannah Montana 20th anniversary special, in which she played the role that made her famous around the world. Cyrus keeps changing who she is, both musically and in terms of style, by balancing nostalgia with reinvention.

articles.title: Selena Gomez’s Petal Pink Nude Mani Redefines Nail Polish
articles.excerpt: Selena Gomez’s petal pink nude mani redefines how to choose nail polish, highlighting a trend toward precise, natural-looking shades.
articles.content_section_1:
Selena Gomez is taking her approach to the beauty world and is now taking it to the world of nail art with her ‘Petal Pink Nude Mani.’ She is proving that choosing the right shade of nail polish is as important as choosing the right foundation for your skin. Just as her Rare Beauty brand’s long-wearing foundation is available in 48 different shades to ensure that you get the perfect match for your skin tone, Selena’s look is also about finding that perfect shade that will complement your natural beauty rather than hiding it.

With her rounded almond shape and sheer pink-toned nude, Gomez’s look is clean and healthy, with a glossy finish that is similar to that of a tinted moisturizer or foundation. The look is also minimalist and sophisticated, making it perfectly in line with the beauty trends for Spring 2026.
articles.content_section_2:
The nail color used is created by celebrity nail artist Tom Bachik. The color used is “FS106,” which is part of his upcoming NEUDES Foundation Series. The series is inspired by makeup, with categories ranging from Foundation, Concealer, to Blush. Each of these products has varying degrees of coverage and color. Foundation colors have balanced shades, concealers have illuminating properties, while blush colors have subtle shades of color.

This is also part of the larger trend in nail art, where ultra-natural nail art is on the rise. Nude nail colors are selected on the basis of their undertone. Gomez’s nail color is a perfect example of how nail color complements the nail. It is effortless, modern, yet classic.

articles.title: Barbie Ferreira’s Oscars Gown Reimagines the Classic Work Shirt as Couture
articles.excerpt: Barbie Ferreira turned a classic Gap Oxford shirt into high fashion at the Oscars 2026, wearing a Zac Posen-designed gown inspired by everyday workwear.
articles.content_section_1:
Barbie Ferreira made a bold statement on the red carpet at the 2026 Oscars by turning an everyday item into high fashion. The actress from Euphoria wore a custom GapStudio gown based on the brand's famous oversized Oxford shirt. This everyday item was given a new look for one of fashion's most glamorous stages.

Zac Posen, serving as Gap's executive vice president and creative director, was the mind behind the indigo dress. It had a structured corseted bodice and a full skirt made of moiré taffeta, which is known for its soft, shiny texture. The outfit took a familiar shape and made it look more polished and ready for the red carpet.
articles.content_section_2:
Posen took direct inspiration from Gap's Original Big Shirt, focusing on how it fit, how simple it was, and how relaxed it was. These parts were turned into couture through careful design choices, like a full button placket running down the skirt, which is similar to the way a classic Oxford shirt is made.

This is an important feature that enables the dress to change. Ferreira decided to keep the buttons closed to create a classic ball gown. The buttons can also be unbuttoned to create a dramatic slit. The design is a blend of functionality and fashion, such that it is difficult to distinguish between sportswear and high fashion.
articles.content_section_3:
Additional elements of style were incorporated to make the idea inspired by the shirt even stronger. The dress includes a tie at the waist, which makes the dress resemble a shirt tied in a knot. This is the feature that gives the design structure and reality. This is the feature that distinguishes modern couture from the rest.

The design is modern and nostalgic at the same time. The design is inspired by the history of Gap on the Oscars' red carpet. Sharon Stone is known to have worn Gap basics with high fashion skirts in the 1990s. It was something nobody thought the actress should wear to the Oscars. Ferreira is carrying on the tradition with her designs, which transform normal clothes into art.

Ferreira's Oscars appearance is evidence of the change in the fashion industry. It is no longer the way it used to be because it appreciates the beauty of simplicity while embracing new ideas. It is the beauty of the design that shows even the simplest clothes can turn into something amazing.

articles.title: All the Best Fashion From The Devil Wears Prada 2 Press Tour
articles.excerpt: From Mexico City to global red carpets, the cast of The Devil Wears Prada 2 is delivering standout fashion moments ahead of the film’s release.
articles.content_section_1:
The long-awaited press tour for The Devil Wears Prada 2 has officially begun, and with it, a new wave of fashion moments that make headlines. Almost two decades have passed since the original film came out, but the sequel is already making waves, not only because of the story but also because of the style statements made by the iconic cast.

Starring Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci, the film revisits the world of high fashion publishing, following Miranda Priestly as she navigates the changing media landscape and faces off against a powerful version of Emily Charlton. As the cast travels the globe promoting the film ahead of its May 1 release, each red carpet appearance is becoming a must-watch fashion event.
articles.content_section_2:
The first stop on the tour in Mexico City was a harbinger of what is to come, and Meryl Streep made a strong entrance in a stunning red Dolce & Gabbana suit, reminiscent of Miranda Priestly’s commanding style with a modern twist. Anne Hathaway, on the other hand, opted for high-fashion drama in a fringed Schiaparelli dress, topped off with a sculptural gold belt for a touch of avant-garde chic.

With more premieres and appearances on the horizon, there is much to look forward to, both for the film itself and for the style moments it will inevitably spark. There is already speculation on what nods to some of the most iconic style moments from the original film, from cerulean sweaters to boots, will make appearances on the tour, and as this tour progresses, a dynamic look at contemporary style through one of cinema’s most stylish franchises is sure to follow.
articles.image1_source: Anne Hathaway in Schiaparelli and Meryl Streep in Dolce & Gabbana

articles.title: Zendaya Says Goodbye to Minimalism With a Bold Statement Skirt
articles.excerpt: Zendaya embraces maximalism in a striking Erdem look, pairing a dramatic feathered skirt with a structured jacket during her New York appearance.
articles.content_section_1:
Zendaya is still the name on everyone’s lips in fashion circles this season, and the actress was spotted out and about in New York sporting an ensemble that was very much against the minimalism and simplicity that has been dominating fashion trends. Despite a busy schedule with various engagements and promotional events, the actress has been consistently delivering fashion statements with the expert guidance of Law Roach.

For a recent appearance on Good Morning America, Zendaya opted to wear an ensemble from Erdem’s fall 2026 collection. The ensemble was a monochromatic theme and featured two major trends from the season: the statement skirt and the funnel-neck jacket.
articles.content_section_2:
While the ensemble was quite bold and statement-worthy, the black tweed jacket provided a much-needed balance to the ensemble. The feathers added a sense of movement and texture to the ensemble. This ensemble is a major departure from Zendaya’s fashion direction over the recent weeks. While she has been sporting bridal-inspired white ensembles from Vivienne Westwood and Louis Vuitton, this ensemble is a welcome respite from the same.

However, while deviating from the all-white theme, the actress ensured a subtle connection to her latest look via her shoes, opting for classic white Christian Louboutin stilettos. This look, in essence, reflects her capability to merge trends with her narrative, thereby proving her position as one of the most influential style icons of the moment.

articles.title: Zendaya’s ‘Something Blue?’ A Couture Ballgown
articles.excerpt: Zendaya stunned at the New York premiere of The Drama in a Schiaparelli ballgown, completing her wedding-themed series of looks with a blue finale dress.
articles.content_section_1:
Zendaya brought glamour to the New York premiere of The Drama in a strapless Schiaparelli ballgown. The gown featured a tiered skirt adorned with black and electric blue ombré feathers and came from Schiaparelli’s spring 2026 couture collection. Completing the look, Tiffany & Co. supplied sapphire-studded earrings and rings.

This finale dress wrapped up a series of outfits inspired by the wedding rhyme: 'something old, something new, something borrowed, something blue.' Zendaya and stylist Law Roach creatively reinterpreted this Victorian superstition, putting their own spin on bridal tradition.
articles.content_section_2:
Earlier in the month, Zendaya showcased her 'something old' at the Los Angeles premiere with a rewear of Vivienne Westwood’s 2015 Oscars gown. Her 'something new' was a custom white Louis Vuitton column dress with a long black train. For 'something borrowed,' Cate Blanchett lent Zendaya a black Armani Privé gown with a plunging, embellished neckline.

Zendaya’s affinity for blue was evident in previous promotional appearances: a flowing seafoam McQueen dress with sky blue and pale pink flowers on Jimmy Kimmel Live! and a navy, boudoir-inspired Zimmermann dress on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon.

articles.title: ‘People Are Mad at Their Exes’: Lily Allen on the Healing Powers of Her West End Girl Tour
articles.excerpt: Lily Allen opens up about heartbreak, healing, and storytelling through music, fashion, and theater during her deeply personal West End Girl tour.
articles.content_section_1:
West End Girl by Lily Allen is an unpolished and highly intimate album created within the period of 16 days during Allen's break up with her ex-husband, actor David Harbour. The album reflects strong emotions such as hurt, anger, and vulnerability that provide space for the listeners to explore their own emotions as well. As the artist herself explains, this stage of grieving process that people tend to skip in reality is represented in the album.

Going on tour with the album has become an important part of her healing process. Allen has been touring the U.K. and North America since March, and because of high demand, he has added more shows to his schedule. The performances have really connected with audiences, many of whom can relate to the themes of heartbreak and healing. Allen says that the fact that so many people have felt these things has made fans feel like they are all healing together.
articles.content_section_2:
The West End Girl tour is very different from her other tours because it focuses on theatrical storytelling. The show was created with Anna Fleische and combines music with stagecraft. It uses props, lighting, and costume changes to create a story that draws the audience in. The songs on the setlist, like “West End Girl,” “Madeline,” and “Pussy Palace,” all add to the emotional arc of the performance.

Improvements based on Allen's experience from her previous live performances have been implemented in the production, which allowed for experimentation and development of a more dramatic and detailed stage appearance. The concert becomes somewhat of a theatrical show, thanks to such creative experiments.
articles.content_section_3:
One cannot deny the important role that fashion plays during this performance. Styling by Mel Ottenberg reflects the emotions and moods of the concert, starting with elegant and classic outfits and transitioning into vulnerable and intimate ones, much like Allen's journey of overcoming heartbreak. Every step of this transition is represented in the costumes, from the tailored Valentino suit featuring lingerie layers to provocative garments such as Self-Portrait bodysuits and 16Arlington leather dress.

Allen has found balance in his life through art, family, and staying sober. She says that touring while sober for the first time was both hard and rewarding, thanks to the support of her family and a close-knit team that traveled with her. The artist embraces both the emotional intensity of her performances and the quieter times of reflection that help her keep going as the tour goes on through November.

articles.title: Selena Gomez Tries Her Own Twist on the Pop of Pink Trend
articles.excerpt: Selena Gomez embraces the ‘pop of pink’ trend in a Prada mini dress, pairing candyfloss hues with playful glam for a standout 2026 fashion moment.
articles.content_section_1:
In 2026, celebrities have been wearing bold "pop of" styles, each with their own unique accent. For example, Harry Styles, Jennifer Lawrence, and Rihanna all wore animal prints, while Kendall Jenner and Katie Holmes wore simple red pops. Hailey Bieber has been wearing buttery yellows, and cobalt blue has been a favorite of Bella Hadid and Greta Lee. Selena Gomez adds her own touch to the colorful mix with a bright pop of pink.

Gomez celebrated the launch of her new matte foundation at a Rare Beauty event in West Hollywood, Los Angeles, where she stood out in a unique outfit. She wore a satin mini dress from Prada's spring 2026 collection. It had a pearl-embellished collar, front pockets, and a delicate white crinoline lining. Erin Walsh styled her hair and added pointed white slingback pumps, diamond hoop earrings, and stacked silver rings to finish off the look. The result was a fun but polished outfit.
articles.content_section_2:
For her makeup, Gomez maintained the pink theme. For her eye makeup, she wore pink eyeshadow coupled with eyeliner that had a perfect flick, pink blush up to her temple areas, and pink lip color. In addition, her hair was done in an elegant chignon, where some strands have been soft framed.

It is clear that Gomez has continued with her love for Prada and feminine yet playful dressing trends, as seen from her past performances in different events in 2025 and her dressing styles in 2026. It can be noted that she has worn her Prada dress at the Golden Globe Awards 2025, and she has used the pink color in most of her dresses and makeup trends.

articles.title: Anne Hathaway’s Prada 2 Looks Inspired by Supernova Joy
articles.excerpt: Anne Hathaway’s Devil Wears Prada 2 looks are inspired by supernova joy, blending bold couture, nostalgia, and modern glamour.
articles.content_section_1:
Excitement is building for The Devil Wears Prada 2, the highly anticipated sequel to the 2006 fashion classic starring Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep. At the film’s first Asia premiere in Tokyo, both stars delivered standout red carpet moments, with Hathaway wearing a strapless Valentino couture gown and Streep appearing in Chanel. For stylist Erin Walsh, who is curating Hathaway’s global press tour looks, the stakes are high—but so is the excitement. Drawing on her own history working at Vogue during the era of the original film, Walsh brings an authentic understanding of the fashion world that defines the franchise.

The relationship that Hathaway and Walsh have built since 2019 is one where they work together creatively through storytelling. It incorporates characteristics from both Hathaway’s own personality as well as her character, Andy Sachs, to create pieces that are reminiscent yet fresh for a new age. The idea is to use clothing that shows the growth of the actress and character over two decades.
articles.content_section_2:
The tour began in Mexico City with two striking fashion moments that set the tone for what’s to come. Hathaway first appeared in a black fringed Schiaparelli dress with a sculptural gold belt, a look inspired by the idea of fashion as art and influenced by the surroundings of Frida Kahlo’s home. She then shifted into a red sequined Stella McCartney dress paired with leather thigh-high boots—an intentional nod to Andy Sachs’s iconic Chanel boot moment from the original film.

These looks highlight Walsh’s strategy of honoring the past while pushing forward into new territory. By experimenting with bold colors, textures, and silhouettes, the styling reflects how the character of Andy has matured while still retaining her fashion-forward sensibility. The balance between tribute and reinvention is central to the visual narrative of the press tour.
articles.content_section_3:
As for the future, Walsh sees the main idea of Hathaway’s wardrobe as “supernova joy,” where boldness and expressiveness of fashion plays an important role as it motivates and empowers. Method dressing is no longer a priority; now the emphasis will be put on the creation of impressive, confidence-inducing looks. There will be some hints of the previous movie, such as cerulean blue, but there will be modern suiting as well because now Andy is a successful journalist.

Walsh also has to take into account the fact that at the same time she needs to style Hathaway for her second film, Mother Mary. In other words, the stylist has to create two completely different images within the same short period of time. Nevertheless, Walsh gladly accepts this challenge, stressing how joyful, creative and collaborative the process with Hathaway has become.

articles.title: Dakota Johnson’s Valentino Cape Defines Red Carpet Trend
articles.excerpt: Dakota Johnson embraces the Valentino cape trend on the red carpet, signaling a shift from sheer styles to elegant, statement outerwear in 2026 fashion.
articles.content_section_1:
Capes, shawls, and intricately trimmed outerwear are currently having a significant moment in fashion, and Dakota Johnson recently showcased this trend at the Time 100 Gala in New York City. While known for her affinity for daring "naked dresses," Johnson opted for a more covered yet equally glamorous Valentino gown featuring a flowing capelet.

The Valentino creation was a cream, Grecian-inspired dress with delicate straps and a draped front, adorned with sequined feather detailing around the neck that fastened to a floor-length cape and train. Paired with diamond-encrusted snake-detail heels, the look evoked a modern Medusa. Johnson’s beauty look complemented the ensemble with feathered bangs, tousled long hair, a smoky brown eye, peachy blush, and a nude lip.
articles.content_section_2:
This is not Johnson's first foray into capes; as a fan of Alessandro Michele's nostalgic designs, she has embraced them across Gucci and now Valentino. The cape trend is enduring, appearing in various forms from boho styles at Chloé to scarf coats at Calvin Klein and Burberry. Other celebrities, including Jennifer Lawrence and Kendall Jenner, have also been spotted in capes. The Time 100 Gala itself saw multiple stars, like Hilary Duff and Kate Hudson, embracing the glamorous silhouette.

This embrace of the cape marks a notable shift for Johnson, who is also a celebrated devotee of the "naked dress," pushing the boundaries of that trend with sheer bodices and crystal-embellished gowns. Her stylist, Kate Young, has noted Johnson's beauty shines in simple, minimalist dresses, requiring no additional adornment. Her everyday style, which often includes brands like The Row, shows that she likes understated elegance. Johnson's recent choice, on the other hand, shows how timeless and sophisticated the red carpet cape is.

articles.title: Kendall Jenner Channels Winged Victory at the 2026 Met Gala
articles.excerpt: Kendall Jenner embraced the 2026 Met Gala’s “Costume Art” theme in a Zac Posen creation inspired by the Winged Victory of Samothrace, blending ancient sculpture with modern couture craftsmanship.
articles.content_section_1:
Inspired by the Winged Victory of Samothrace

Kendall Jenner is still making a name for herself at the Met Gala, taking inspiration from the “Costume Art” theme through an awe-inspiring ensemble that draws inspiration from the Winged Victory of Samothrace. In her twelfth year attending this renowned annual event, Kendall collaborated with fashion designer Zac Posen, who designed her look and is good friends with her family.

The stunning ensemble draws inspiration from the legendary Greek statue, which greets visitors at the Louvre with its commanding presence. Despite never having collaborated before, Kendall and Zac shared mutual admiration. She fondly recalls Christina Ricci’s 2011 Met Gala dress—the “spider dress”—as a look that’s stayed with her, saying, “It’s so perfect, and she looks so gorgeous and hot.”

Zac Posen, who has a longstanding connection with Kendall’s sister Kim Kardashian, had been eager to work with Kendall for years. When the 2026 theme was announced, he reached out with a heartfelt letter, expressing his desire to create something extraordinary together. Kendall, captivated by the idea, admits she was thinking of Zac when the opportunity arose. “It felt very fated,” she shares, believing the universe played a hand in bringing their vision to life.
articles.content_section_2:
Transforming Ancient Art Into Modern Fashion

The designer reimagined the ancient statue with a modern twist, starting with a classic white Gap T-shirt—an iconic staple—then transforming it into a sculptural, glamorous masterpiece. “I took a white Gap T-shirt and started pulling and tweaking it to interpret the sculpture in a contemporary way,” Zac explains. Drawing inspiration from legendary figures of jersey and drapery—Madame Grès, Alaïa, Roy Halston, Vionnet, and others—he crafted a look that balances timeless art with modern elegance.

Kendall was deeply involved throughout the creative process, eager to embody the rippling, windswept qualities of the statue’s tunic. Zac selected a lightweight cotton-viscose blend to create a wet, fluid drapery effect, layering it with satin-faced chiffon and organza to evoke the sculpture’s fluidity and movement. “It’s been incredible to see Zac’s creative process unfold,” Kendall says. “I love being part of every step.”

The base of the dress features a custom leather corset, designed by Abel Cepeda Ljoka and Will Kowall of Seks NY, who 3D-printed Kendall’s body to craft a precise mannequin and bust. Zac draped the fabric over this sculpted foundation, emphasizing the garment’s sculptural details and adding a touch of allure with an exposed corset.
articles.content_section_3:
Wings, Movement, and Met Gala Magic

No homage to Nike is complete without wings. Kendall envisioned them as effortless and lightweight, so Zac crafted the wings from airy satin-faced chiffon. There are large scale photo prints which are based on the real wings of the victory of Samothrace which create a sense of grandeur and movement. As Kendall lifts her arms on the red carpet, the wings will fan out dramatically, creating a stunning visual impact.

A seasoned Met Gala attendee, Kendall has her routine down to a science—watching the livestream before arriving, then mingling and soaking in the moment. Yet, even after 12 years, she reveals she still gets pre-event jitters: “I’ll probably need a shot before the carpet,” she laughs.

This year’s look is a perfect blend of ancient art and modern fashion—an unforgettable tribute to the timeless power of costume art.

articles.title: 2026 Cannes Film Festival Opens With Star Power and Red Carpet Glamour
articles.excerpt: The 2026 Cannes Film Festival officially began in France with a dazzling opening ceremony, historic jury appointments, and standout fashion moments from stars like Demi Moore and Philippine Leroy-Beaulieu.
articles.content_section_1:
Cannes 2026 Officially Begins

Tonight marks the official kickoff of the 2026 Cannes Film Festival in France, and the event’s iconic red carpet is already shining with star power. The festival’s opening ceremony took place at the Grand Théâtre Lumière, hosted by French-Malian actress Eye Haïdara. The ceremony introduced this year’s esteemed jury, led by Park Chan-wook—the first Korean jury president in Cannes history—joined by stars like Demi Moore, Ruth Negga, Laura Wandel, Chloé Zhao, Diego Céspedes, Isaach De Bankolé, Paul Laverty, and Stellan Skarsgård.

The event culminated with a performance by Theodora and Oklou, followed by the screening of the opening film, The Electric Venus by Pierre Salvadori.
articles.content_section_2:
Red Carpet Fashion Takes Center Stage

On the first day of Cannes, the red carpet was packed with fashion highlights from A-list guests. Among the standout looks: Philippine Leroy-Beaulieu from Emily in Paris in a flowing purple Saint Laurent gown by Antony Vaccarello, and Demi Moore in a sleek, sparkling Jacquemus dress.

This is just the beginning—stay tuned for two weeks of unforgettable fashion moments. Scroll down for all the top style highlights from the 2026 Cannes Film Festival.

articles.title: Netflix Documentary Explores Microplastics and Health Risks
articles.excerpt: Netflix’s ‘The Plastic Detox’ explores the impact of microplastics on health, fertility, and daily life while highlighting sustainable innovations.
articles.content_section_1:
On March 16, Netflix will release a new documentary called "The Plastic Detox". The movie looks into the growing concern about microplastics, especially their possible links to lower fertility rates and other health issues that may come up later.

Microplastics have also been found in clothing fibers and cosmetics, in addition to food packaging and toys. The word describes small pieces of plastic that can come apart from bigger pieces over time and be eaten or breathed in. More and more studies have found a connection between plastics, which often have chemicals added during the manufacturing process, and health problems, from infertility to cancer.

The documentary also looks at whether making small changes to your lifestyle can help lower your exposure and improve your health in general.
articles.content_section_2:
With the help of environmental and reproductive epidemiologist Shanna Swan, the documentary follows six couples who are having trouble getting pregnant for no clear reason. Over the course of three months, they try to cut down on their exposure to chemicals that come from plastic in the hopes of getting healthier.

The film features voices from community organizers opposing petrochemical projects, alongside fashion designers striving to eliminate harmful dyes and synthetic fibers from their manufacturing methods.

The documentary connects environmental problems with personal health, showing how plastics are everywhere in modern life.
articles.content_section_3:
LVMH-Backed Sparxell Showcased in Documentary for Innovative Plant-Based Pigment Solutions

One of the documentary's highlights is its focus on Sparxell, a company developing plant-based, biodegradable pigments. Sparxell is working to supplant conventional petrochemical dyes, mined minerals, and plastic-based pigments—think of the widely used azo dyes—with assistance from LVMH.

The company's cellulose-based pigments are already being used in business, and there are several partnerships and pilot projects going on. The documentary also shows how designers like Patrick McDowell have worked with Sparxell and textile innovators to make plant-based alternatives for making clothes.The movie highlights these new ideas to show how they could help make plastics less harmful to the environment and people's health.

By spotlighting these innovations, the film underscores the potential for sustainable solutions to reduce the environmental and health impacts of plastics.

articles.title: Yves Saint Laurent Onstage Exhibition in Marrakech
articles.excerpt: The Yves Saint Laurent Onstage exhibition in Marrakech explores the designer’s passion for theatre, ballet, and performance through iconic works.
articles.content_section_1:
The Yves Saint Laurent Onstage exhibition was opened in the Musée Yves Saint Laurent Marrakech in late January. The exhibition is centered around Yves Saint Laurent's work in theater, music halls, and ballet. The exhibition will be open until January 5, 2027. It provides a new perspective on a lesser-known but important side of Yves Saint Laurent's work.

This exhibition features Yves Saint Laurent's lasting love for the performing arts, which was sparked in him when he was just thirteen years old in Oran, Algeria. Curated by Stephan Janson and Domitille Éblé from the Musée Yves Saint Laurent Paris, the show explores how stage performance influenced his artistic vision. Building on the 2024 Rome exhibition, the Marrakech edition features an ambitious set design and previously unseen pieces, using items from the Fondation Pierre Bergé - Yves Saint Laurent archives and international collections.
articles.content_section_2: Saint Laurent is known to be a visionary costume designer. He revolutionized women's fashion by highlighting movement, freedom, and character expression. The artistic works created were not only for decoration but were incorporated into the performances and became a vital part of the artists' identities. The works ensured that artists such as Sylvie Vartan and Johnny Hallyday became more captivating on stage, which is what led to their legendary success.
articles.content_section_3:
The exhibition offers visitors a realistic setting to walk around and view original costumes, sketches, and set designs, including rare archival pieces. Some of the works were created by the famous Karinska workshops, thus showing how sketches can be transformed into living costumes inspired by Léon Bakst's bright colors.

The Yves Saint Laurent museum has been open since 2017 and is located in the heart of Marrakech, near the Jardin Majorelle. It continues to honor the designer's lasting cultural impact.

articles.title: Bad Bunny Debuts Custom Zara at Super Bowl 2026 Halftime
articles.excerpt: Bad Bunny made history at Super Bowl 2026 wearing custom Zara during halftime, marking a cultural and strategic moment for Inditex in the U.S.
articles.content_section_1:
Inditex showed how important the US is as a growth market when its main brand, Zara, dressed halftime performer Bad Bunny at Super Bowl LX on February 8, 2026. The event, which took place at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, drew millions of viewers and put Zara at the center of one of the world's biggest cultural events.

The Seattle Seahawks won the game against the New England Patriots, but the Puerto Rican superstar stole the show at halftime. Bad Bunny gave a performance that mixed music with a strong cultural message, just like Michael Jackson, Beyoncé, and Kendrick Lamar.
articles.content_section_2:
Bad Bunny, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, opened with his hit "Tití Me Preguntó." He turned the stage into a tribute to Puerto Rican culture, complete with images of sugarcane fields. Lady Gaga and Ricky Martin, two of the biggest stars in the world, were also there, which made the show even more appealing to people all over the world.

The artist ended the show with a touchdown, and the football was emblazoned with the phrase "Together, We Are America." This is a very powerful symbol that emphasizes unity and Hispanic-American history. The performance combined cultural pride with a political statement.

Being featured by Zara was a big deal for the fashion world because Zara is one of the few Spanish brands that has designed for a halftime show for the Super Bowl. Zara designed two custom off-white ensembles for Bad Bunny.
articles.content_section_3:
The first look, called the "Varsity" look, had a jersey sweatshirt, pants, and tie that were inspired by the NFL. The number 64 and the name Ocasio were on the shirt as a tribute to his mother. The second suit, called a "Power" suit, had big shoulders and loose seams. It was worn during the main part of the performance.

The single color scheme reflected Caribbean influence and helped to reinforce the artist's message of unity. The Adidas "BadBo 1.0" was the artist's first original shoe shape for the brand and completed the look.

The Inditex deal was more than just a fashion collaboration for the retailer; it was a shrewd business move to increase its cultural and business footprint in the U.S., which has been its largest market outside of Spain since 2021.

articles.title: Charlie Puth Enters His Dad Music Era with Whatever’s Clever
articles.excerpt: Charlie Puth embraces his 'Dad Music Era' with Whatever’s Clever, blending nostalgia, authenticity, and yacht-rock inspired sound.
articles.content_section_1:
Charlie Puth is stepping into a brand-new era—and it’s not what you’d expect from the pop hitmaker behind chart-toppers like “See You Again.” This time, it’s personal.
